task_result.final_answer:
The most recently created open issue is "SDK DX follow-up: unify client + orchestrator entrypoint" in the "Backlog" status.

Here are the details:
- **ID**: ENG-276
- **Title**: SDK DX follow-up: unify client + orchestrator entrypoint
- **Description**: ## Context

`#239` shipped hybrid routing safely by adding `createKontextOrchestrator` and keeping `createKontextClient` behavior unchanged.

That solved runtime capability, but DX/structure is still split:

*   users see two entrypoints for one client use case
*   orchestrator code is outside `src/client` even though it is a client-side variant

## Problem

Most SDK users want one client API that just works. They should not need to pick “client” vs… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

- **Status**: Backlog
- **Created By**: michel@cobrowser.xyz
- **Team**: Kontext

claims: The response includes documentation about workflows
tasks.prompt: Search Linear documentation for information about workflows.

task_result.final_answer:
Based on the search results, here's a summary of how workflows are handled in Linear:

### Team-Specific Workflows

The primary way to configure workflows in Linear is at the team level through **Issue Statuses**. Each team can have its own unique workflow, which defines the stages an issue moves through from creation to completion.

You can customize these workflows by going to **Settings > Teams > Issue statuses & automations**. Here you can:

*   **Add, edit, or remove statuses**: Tailor the workflow to your team's specific needs.
*   **Set a default status**: Define the initial state for new issues.
*   **Configure auto-close and auto-archive**: Automatically close or archive issues that haven't been updated for a set period.

For more details, you can refer to the [Issue status documentation](https://linear.app/docs/configuring-workflows).

### Personal Workflow Automation

You can also personalize your own workflow and speed up your processes through your **Preferences**. This includes settings for:

*   **Auto-assigning issues to yourself** when you create them or move them to a "started" status.
*   **Git-related automations**, such as automatically changing an issue's status when you copy a git branch name.

You can find these settings in the [Preferences documentation](https://linear.app/docs/account-preferences).

### Integration-Based Workflows

Linear's integrations with other tools can also be a key part of your workflow:

*   **GitLab/GitHub**: You can automate your development workflow by linking Linear issues to merge/pull requests. For example, you can configure issues to automatically update when a merge request is drafted, opened, or merged. Read more in the [GitLab documentation](https://linear.app/docs/gitlab).
*   **Figma**: For design workflows, you can use the Linear plugin for Figma to create and link issues directly from your design files. You can also embed Figma designs into Linear issues. More information is available in the [Figma documentation](https://linear.app/docs/figma).

You can discover more integrations to improve your workflow in the [Integration Directory](https://linear.app/docs/integration-directory).

tool_calls.result: {"content":[{"type":"resource","resource":{"uri":"inline://execute-tool","mimeType":"application/json","text":"{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"96e92cf0-7141-4395-8660-393a8c6a9ddd\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Preferences\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/account-preferences\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Adjust your preferences to help you move faster and personalize your workflow.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nGeneral\\\\n\\\\nThe general preferences offer high-level adjustments for how you want things to appear in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nDefault home view\\\\n\\\\nThis setting lets you set the default view that opens whenever you close or log out of Linear and re-open/re-login. It defaults to your team's Active issues view, which is the view that shows all issues that are in an Unstarted or Started workflow status.\\\\n\\\\nTo change your default home view, open the dropdown menu to select from any of these default views:\\\\n\\\\nAll issues\\\\n\\\\nActive issues\\\\n\\\\nCurrent cycle\\\\n\\\\nInbox\\\\n\\\\nMy Issues\\\\n\\\\nFavorited Views\\\\n\\\\nFavorited Projects\\\\n\\\\nDisplay full names\\\\n\\\\nSelect whether you want to see your team members' full names or the username they have personalized in their Profile section.\\\\n\\\\nFirst day of the week\\\\n\\\\nPersonalize how you want your calendars to display your weeks.\\\\n\\\\nConvert text emoticons into emojis\\\\n\\\\nTurn this toggle on to speed up emoji input.\\\\n\\\\nInterface and theme\\\\n\\\\nAdjust your font size and/or opt for a pointer cursor when hovering over interactive elements.\\\\n\\\\nChoose from our presets or create your own custom theme for your workspace. Select several Light or Dark modes as a preference or choose system preference if you'd like the app to switch based on your computer settings.\\\\n\\\\nYou can also build your own custom theme. Find and use over 70 themes (and counting!) on the open source site linear.style, where you can click to copy and use them. \\\\n\\\\nDesktop application\\\\n\\\\nChoose whether to attempt to open Linear URLs in the desktop app.\\\\n\\\\nSelect whether you want notification badges on your desktop app icon, or remove them to avoid the noise.\\\\n\\\\nChoose whether to enable spell check.\\\\n\\\\nAutomations and workflows\\\\n\\\\nAuto-assign to self\\\\n\\\\nOptionally, enable an automation to always assign issues you create to yourself. You can also configure whether you should be auto-assigned to issues you move to a started status.\\\\n\\\\nIf you don't turn these on, you can always toggle the Create more button or use Cmd/Ctrl Shift Enter when creating issues to create more with the same assignee. We don't have a setting to auto-assign issues to specific users. The best way to accomplish that workflow is to create templates with the assignee field set to a specific user.\\\\n\\\\nGit attachment format\\\\n\\\\nDisplay title, or title and repository for your git attachments.\\\\n\\\\nGit-related automations\\\\n\\\\nIf you've enabled Linear's GitHub or GitLab integrations, you'll likely copy the git branch name with Cmd/Ctrl Shift . to link Linear issues to pull requests. You can set two actions to happen whenever you do:\\\\n\\\\nOn git branch copy, move issue to started status which moves the issue to the first workflow status under the started category (often \\\\\\\"In progress\\\\\\\"). \\\\n\\\\nOn git branch copy, auto-assign to yourself\\\\n\\\\nWe highly recommend these settings for any developers using Linear. In combination, they save you a few clicks every time you begin an issue.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"8d26616f-28d7-4210-af91-79da93ea773a\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"GitLab\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/gitlab\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Linear supports linking your Linear issues to GitLab and automates your merge request workflows.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nLink Linear issues to GitLab merge requests. Automate your MR workflow so that issues update when MRs are drafted, opened, merged and when reviews are requested. You can link single or multiple issues to a specific MR.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure the GitLab integration\\\\n\\\\nWe support linking GitLab merge requests for both hosted and self-hosted installations, as long as your installation is publicly available. \\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Linear Settings > Features > Integrations > GitLab\\\\n\\\\nClick Enable to launch the set-up pop-up.\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to GitLab > User Settings > Personal Access Token or GitLab > Project Settings > Access Token to create an API access token.\\\\n\\\\nAn access token is used to query the GitLab API for further information and also used to post issue linkbacks. Because GitLab doesn't support bot accounts, linkbacks are created under the name of the token owner. It's recommended that you create a new user for Linear to act as the bot account.\\\\n\\\\nWhen creating the token, set the api or read_api scope.\\\\n\\\\nIf the read_api scope is selected, Linear will not post linkbacks to the issue on GitLab merge requests. If using a project access token, the token needs Reporter role or higher.\\\\n\\\\n(Optional) Enable and set a custom URL for your self-hosted GitLab installation without any path (e.g. https://gitlab.yourcompany.com). This URL needs to be accessible to public Internet and is only required for self-hosted installations. The earliest supported version of GitLab is 15.6.\\\\n\\\\nIf you need to grant access to Linear's IPs, they are 35.231.147.226, 35.243.134.228, 34.140.253.14, 34.38.87.206, 34.134.222.122, and 35.222.25.142.\\\\n\\\\nClick Connect.\\\\n\\\\nLinear will generate the Webhook URL after it validates the access token. Copy and paste this URL to GitLab in either:\\\\n\\\\nA Group's Webhook Settings (For a company on GitLab's Premium or Ultimate tiers) to integrate all projects under it.\\\\n\\\\nA Project's Webhook Settings, to individually connect a specific project.\\\\n\\\\nEnable the following Triggers:\\\\n\\\\nPush events\\\\n\\\\nComments\\\\n\\\\nMerge request events\\\\n\\\\nPipeline events\\\\n\\\\nUnder SSL verification, ensure Enable SSL verification is checked\\\\n\\\\nClick Save changes.\\\\n\\\\nLink Merge Requests\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new branch\\\\n\\\\nSet the branch format in Linear Settings > Workspace > Integrations GitLab > Branch format. When viewing or selecting a Linear issue, use the Copy git branch name action or shortcut Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + . It will give you a branch name with the issue ID which you can use to create a new branch in GitLab.\\\\n\\\\nAdd the issue ID in the MR title\\\\n\\\\nInclude the Linear issue ID (e.g. ID-123) in the MR title when creating merge requests.\\\\n\\\\nUse a magic word\\\\n\\\\nAdd a magic word + issue ID in the MR description (e.g. Fixes ENG-123). The integration cannot link MRs via comments or commit messages. The available magic words to link issues are: close, closes, closed, closing fix, fixes, fixed, fixing resolve, resolves, resolved, resolving complete, completes, completed, completing.\\\\n\\\\nTo link MRs to issues without them automating your issue status on MR merge, use the one of the contributing magic words: ref, references, part of, related to, contributes to, towards. When using a non-closing magic word like this, the linked MR or commit will still move the issue through other statuses per Workflow settings, but will not automate the issue's status when the MR or commit merges. Instead, it will only transition the issue through to what’s configured prior to the On MR or commit merge event.\\\\n\\\\nLink multiple Linear issues to an MR\\\\n\\\\nTo link multiple Linear issues to a single Merge Request, or to link a Merge Request after creating it, use the magic word linking method by including multiple closing statements in the MR description (e.g. Fixes ENG-123, DES-5 and ENG-256). Linking will happen after you save your changes. The Linear issue will not close until all Merge Requests have been closed/merged.\\\\n\\\\nMerge Request automation\\\\n\\\\nThe merge request automation allows you to select specific statuses for your Linear issues based on MR changes—saving you the hassle of updating Linear issues manually. \\\\n\\\\nCustomize the MR automation in Settings > Team > Workflow. Since this is a team setting, it must be configured for each team in your workspace.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nReady for merge automation\\\\n\\\\nIf you'd like to use the Merge Request automation to capture passing CI checks, please verify in GitLab that:\\\\n\\\\nYou have enabled Pipeline events under the Webhook settings for any repo part of the GitLab integration.\\\\n\\\\nAll pipelines that Linear should consider when determining if an MR is mergeable need to be merge request pipelines. GitLab documentation is available here.\\\\n\\\\nUnder ‘Merge requests’ in project settings, ‘Pipelines must succeed’ is checked.\\\\n\\\\nCustom merge queues\\\\n\\\\nIf you use a custom merge queue that merges changes and then closes the merge request, be sure to add the externally-merged label before closing it. This tells Linear to treat the merge request as successfully merged even though it was closed.\\\\n\\\\nBranch-specific rules\\\\n\\\\nYou can set custom workflow automations based on particular target branches. For example, you can now configure that when a MR is merged to:\\\\n\\\\nstaging, the issue status should change to “In QA”\\\\n\\\\nmain, the issue status should change to “Deployed”\\\\n\\\\nYou can also override a default rule in a particular branch with “no action” if desired, so that issues linked to a change in that branch will not change status. Branch rules can be specified using regex, e.g: ^fea/.* can set automations for all feature branches.\\\\n\\\\nIssue linkbacks\\\\n\\\\nWhen an issue is linked with a merge request, Linear posts a linkback message as a comment with the issue title and description. All the merge requests are also listed in the issue details in Linear. When your MR is being reviewed in GitLab, see the avatars of up to three most recent reviewers and their review states without leaving Linear. This cross-referencing makes it faster to retain context without jumping between apps. Linkbacks are managed by the scope selected.\\\\n\\\\nAuto-assign and update status\\\\n\\\\nSave yourself a few steps by toggling on our automations in Settings > My Account > Preferences > Behavior.\\\\n\\\\nAuto-assigns the issue to you, and/or\\\\n\\\\nMove the issue to the first Started status (which you can customize in Settings > Team > Workflow) when you copy the git branch name.\\\\n\\\\nAuto-linking\\\\n\\\\nGitLab supports rendering URLs pointing to Linear when you mention issue IDs in GitLab. To configure this functionality, please reference GitLab's documentation. \\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"87abf250-960d-4a50-85cf-c67aa69ded11\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Issue status\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/configuring-workflows\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Set statuses that your issues will move through on each team.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nIssues statuses define the type and order of states that issues can move through from start to completion. These workflows are team-specific and come with a default set and order: Backlog > Todo > In Progress > Done > Canceled. \\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nAdd or edit statuses in a team from Settings -> Teams -> Issue statuses & automations. You will see a list of all statuses in that team and their order. Click the three dots next to each status and select Edit to make changes to the name, color, or description. Create statuses by clicking the + button, or remove statuses (as long as at least one status exists in each category). \\\\n\\\\nStatuses can be rearranged within a category but categories cannot be moved around. To change the order of a status, drag it to a new position.\\\\n\\\\nDefault status\\\\n\\\\nThe default status defines the workflow status that will be applied to newly created issues in your team. This can be overridden by the user when creating an issue, but makes it a lot easier to track and organize new issues that come in. By default, your first Backlog status will be the default status. To change that, hover over a different status in the Backlog or Todo categories and then select Make default.\\\\n\\\\nDuplicate issue status\\\\n\\\\nWhen you mark an issue as a duplicate of another, its status will change to Canceled. You can create a custom status in the Canceled category (e.g. Duplicate) and then configure workflow settings so that the custom status is applied instead. \\\\n\\\\nTriage\\\\n\\\\nTriage is an additional status category that acts as an Inbox for your team. Triage is particularly powerful when combined with other integrations like Asks, Slack, or our support ticketing integrations. Learn more here.\\\\n\\\\nAuto-close and auto-archive\\\\n\\\\nWhen enabled, auto-close will close issues that have not been updated in a set period of time.\\\\n\\\\nAuto-archive controls when issues in the team will be archived. When an issue archives, its creator will be notified—this is an opportunity to unarchive if the issue is still relevant. Archived issues are still searchable and restorable in the future.\\\\n\\\\nArchiving is only automatic and is not available as a manual action. More information about archiving is available here.\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"ce5ff110-a02b-450e-be48-1fcddba7dbc9\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Integration Directory\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/integration-directory\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Discover Linear add-ons or build your own\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nLinear's Integration Directory features apps and add-ons created by the Linear team as well as external applications. Install these to improve your workflow and sync with your favorite tools. You can also build your own integrations and submit them to the directory.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nLinear crafted\\\\n\\\\nIf you see an integration with a star badge on the icon, that means it was crafted by the Linear team. You can install them via the link in the directory or by going to Settings > Workspace > Integrations and then select the integration name. \\\\n\\\\nFor most integrations, you'll have to be a workspace admin to install them for your workspace. If you don't know who is an admin, go to Settings > Workspace > Members and filter for Admins.\\\\n\\\\nThird-party integrations\\\\n\\\\nThe directory also features integrations built by other apps and third parties. We recommend doing your own research into the integration owner and permissions required before installing these integrations. You can find the creator's website and contact in the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nBuild your own\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear's API to build your own integration and submit it to the directory following the instructions below. We recommend building applications using OAuth and having a separate workspace for the application, which gives all admins access to the application (instead of only the application creator).\\\\n\\\\nWe'll accept integrations that we think are useful to the community and are built by formal companies. We generally do not accept scripts or apps built by hobbyists, but feel free to reach out to integrations@linear.app if you think it should be included. You can also ask questions and see what others are building in our Slack community's #api channel.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit your integration\\\\n\\\\nFill out this form. It includes a sample page to give you a sense of copy style and length.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit assets to integrations@linear.app or include a link in the form. We've built a template in Figma to make this easy.\\\\n\\\\nSend any questions to integrations@linear.app\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"c79e8be3-1aed-4abb-9a69-28f592fcbe89\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Figma\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/figma\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"We use Figma at Linear and our integrated tools make it easy for you to collaborate on design.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nThere are two ways to connect Figma to Linear to streamline your design and development workflows. The Linear plugin for Figma allows you to create and link issues directly from Figma. A separate integration enables you to embed designs into Linear descriptions, comments and documents. \\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nEmbed Figma previews in Linear\\\\n\\\\nConnect to Figma under Settings > Features > Integrations > Figma. We recommend that you do this in a browser and not the desktop client. Once connected, the integration will work across your full workspace.\\\\n\\\\nLinear plugin for Figma\\\\n\\\\nTo create or link issues from Figma, select a frame, section or link and run the Linear plugin from Resources > Plugins > Linear or run directly from the Figma community here.\\\\n\\\\nEmbed Figma Previews\\\\n\\\\nPreview designs\\\\n\\\\nSimply copy a link to specific frames or files and paste it into a Linear issue description or comment. We automatically convert the link into a design preview. Even if the file changes, the snapshot in Linear remains as it appeared when the comment or description was created. \\\\n\\\\nOpen files\\\\n\\\\nClick to preview the embedded file without having to leave Linear. We currently support interactive in-app preview of publicly shared Figma files only. We're considering building support for private files in the future. We built this as a custom integration using Figma's API with their OAuth2 authentication instead of using their standard embed to create a faster experience.\\\\n\\\\nRefresh files\\\\n\\\\nBy default, we do not refresh the Figma preview so that you maintain the context of related comments and issue descriptions. To update the preview, go into Edit mode in the issue or comment, then hover over the Figma file. You'll see a Refresh button appear, which you can click to pull the latest version. This action cannot be undone.\\\\n\\\\nLinear Plugin for Figma\\\\n\\\\nRun the plugin to see all Linear issues linked to your designs. From the plugin, select issues or the linked elements below the issue description to be taken to the inked frame, section, or page.\\\\n\\\\nCreate issues\\\\n\\\\nWhen using the Linear plugin from within Figma, select the + button to create issues that link pages, frames, and sections of your Figma design. You'll be able to create issues in any teams that you've joined. \\\\n\\\\nLink issues\\\\n\\\\nLink designs to existing issues by searching for the ID, title, or description. From the Linear side, you can link designs to issues by attaching Figma links for the page, section, or frame using Ctrl L. You can link multiple issues to the same design and multiple frames, sections, or pages to a single Linear issue. \\\\n\\\\nUpdate issues\\\\n\\\\nUpdate properties such as team, status, assignee, and project directly from Figma. You'll be able to update these at any point during your design process. Any changes made in Linear are immediately synced to the Figma plugin.\\\\n\\\\nFilter and sort\\\\n\\\\nFilter your issues that appear in the plugin window to hide completed or cancelled issues, show only assigned or created by you, and to sort by status, priority, nam, or created date. By default, the plugin will hide canceled issues.\\\\n\\\\nFigma Plugin \\\\n\\\\nPrivacy Policy\\\\n\\\\nTerms of Service By authenticating the plugin, you agree to Linear's Terms of Service. Plugin authentication grants you access to Linear's features and functionality.\\\\n\\\\nFigma File Key Transmission When the plugin is opened within Figma, Linear will receive the file key for the current Figma file. When connecting a frame in Figma to a Linear issue, Linear will receive and securely store the file key associated with the respective Figma file. This storage of file keys allows Linear to establish the necessary connection and enable bi-directional linking between Figma and Linear.\\\\n\\\\nFigma File Key Access Any user with access to a Linear issue will also have access to the associated file key for any linked Figma frames.\\\\n\\\\nData Removal Users have the authority to remove Figma data stored by Linear at any time. This can be achieved by either disconnecting the frame from the Linear issue or deleting the associated team or workspace. To permanently delete your data from Linear’s storage contact security@linear.app\\\\n\\\\nData Processing All data, including Figma file keys, is processed by Linear in accordance with Linear’s Data Processing Agreement (DPA). The DPA outlines the measures taken by Linear to ensure the privacy and security of customer data. For more details on our data processing practices, please refer to our DPA.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}]\"}]}"}}]}

claims: The response includes documentation about creating issues
tasks.prompt: Search Linear docs for how to create an issue.

task_result.final_answer:
Based on the search results, here's how you can create an issue in Linear:

**From Slack:**

You can create Linear issues directly from Slack messages.
* **Using @linear:** Mention `@linear` in a Slack channel to have the Linear agent create an issue based on the conversation's context. For example: `@linear file a bug, assign me`.
* **From a message:** Use the "More actions" menu on a Slack message to create a Linear issue.
* **Using templates:** You can use your existing Linear issue templates when creating issues from Slack.

**From other integrations:**

Linear also supports creating issues from other platforms:
* **Customer Support Tools:** When an issue is created from integrations with **Intercom, Zendesk, or Front**, it will automatically add a request pointing back to the source.
* **Salesforce:** You can create or link to issues from Salesforce cases.

tool_calls.name: search_documentation
tool_calls.result: {"content":[{"type":"resource","resource":{"uri":"inline://execute-tool","mimeType":"application/json","text":"{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"d4a4f7b8-87ab-4afd-8b49-a6ec4a8e62b9\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Slack\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/slack\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Combine Linear with Slack to keep everyone in sync.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from Slack messages, sync threads between Slack and Linear, set up personal and channel-specific notifications, display rich unfurls in Slack and more.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nIntegration setup\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Features > Integrations > Slack to connect your Slack account to Linear. You must be a Linear admin to do this. \\\\n\\\\nOnce this has been completed, others in your Linear workspace can:\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear agent by mentioning @Linear\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from the More actions menu on Slack messages or by using the /linear command on all plans\\\\n\\\\nTake quick actions from Linear links shared in your Slack workspace\\\\n\\\\nView rich unfurls in Slack that show key issue, comment, document, initiative or project details\\\\n\\\\nEnable personal Slack notifications\\\\n\\\\nSend team and project updates to dedicated Slack channels\\\\n\\\\nNotifications setup\\\\n\\\\nSlack notifications are available for teams, projects, and individuals. Once the initial integration has been configured, any user can set up these notifications. \\\\n\\\\nConnect multiple Slack workspaces\\\\n\\\\nLinear's Enterprise plan supports connecting multiple Slack workspaces to Linear to use the Slack integration. If you're using Slack's Enterprise Grid plan for example, this would allow you to use the Slack integration across your workspaces. To add a new workspace, go to Linear's Slack integration settings and click the + button under connected workspaces. You must hold Admin permissions on an Enterprise Linear plan in order to enable this feature.\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent for Slack\\\\n\\\\nMention @linear in discussions on Slack, and the Linear agent will create issues informed by your conversation's context. Use natural language to specify issue details or simply let the agent infer what's needed.\\\\n\\\\nFor example, try:\\\\n\\\\n@linear file a bug, assign me\\\\n\\\\n@linear make feature requests from this thread\\\\n\\\\n@linear file to the Rideshare Loyalty project\\\\n\\\\nTo use this functionality, send /invite @linear to the desired Slack channel before sending. This feature is available to users in your Slack workspace with Linear accounts.\\\\n\\\\nIn settings, you can choose to allow Slack’s workflow builder to mention @Linear agent to support automating actions.\\\\n\\\\nSet Linear agent guidance\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent considers instructions you write in Slack integration settings on how to create issues. Use this field to refine the agent's behavior and give it more context about how you use Slack. \\\\n\\\\nYou might give it context about your channel naming structure and how it relates to your Linear projects, what statuses you prefer it create in, the team it should use when unsure, and more.\\\\n\\\\nOutside of this field, the agent also uses contextual clues to help infer where to create issues (for example, if you're sending project updates from a project to a channel that sounds related, issues created in that channel will favor creation in that project.)\\\\n\\\\nCreate issues with message actions\\\\n\\\\nYou can also create issues using the More actions menu on a Slack message if you prefer to specify all the details of your created issue.\\\\n\\\\nIf you select a team in the resulting window that uses default templates, that template's text will appear in the description field. Only Linear users in your Slack workspace can create issues with this integration. \\\\n\\\\nUse templates\\\\n\\\\nYour issue templates in Linear can also be used in Slack. Add templates to your Slack integration from workspace template settings or the Slack settings page. Admins can make up to 10 issue templates available in your Slack integration, which any Linear members in your Slack workspace can view and apply during issue creation. If you have a default template set for your team, it’ll show up as an additional template option after the team has been selected.\\\\n\\\\nTemplates in private teams are not available to the Slack integration (nor in other integrations that support templates like Intercom and Zendesk.) If you need to allow users to create issues using templates in private teams, consider using Asks where this use-case is supported.\\\\n\\\\nSync threads\\\\n\\\\nTo create a synced thread, create an issue from Slack through the More actions menu on a Slack message. \\\\n\\\\nWhen you click Submit, you'll also create a synced comment thread in the Linear issue by default. Both threads will update as replies are sent in either location, and we'll also update the synced thread in Slack when the issue is completed or canceled, or reopened after being completed or canceled.\\\\n\\\\nWhen people in your company report issues in Slack, syncing threads is a great way to keep them in the loop regardless of whether they're in your Linear workspace. Comments made in the synced Linear thread will also appear in Slack, and the Slack thread will be updated when the issue is completed or canceled. \\\\n\\\\nIf an issue synced to a Slack thread is marked as a duplicate of another issue, we'll also update the Slack thread where the duplicate was created once the canonical issue is resolved.\\\\n\\\\nAdd Slack messages to existing issues\\\\n\\\\nThere are a few options to link Slack messages to existing Linear issues.\\\\n\\\\nNotifications functionality\\\\n\\\\nRich unfurls and issue actions \\\\n\\\\nOnce you've connected the integration for Slack, we'll show expanded links anytime you post issue, project, document, or initiative links from public teams in Slack.\\\\n\\\\n URLs associated with private Linear teams never unfurl. Unfurling can be disabled in Settings > Integrations > Slack if desired.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"157c00c4-0d54-4965-a2d3-fa7ad0511529\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Insights\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/insights\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Real-time analytics for all your Linear data. Visualize issue data to spot trends and remove blockers.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nInsights turns your Linear issues into a dataset you can analyze to better understand how you work. Use Insights to answer questions like:\\\\n\\\\nWhat projects take up the most resources? \\\\n\\\\nHow quickly do you fix bugs? \\\\n\\\\nHow consistently do you prioritize issues? \\\\n\\\\nDo you estimate accurately? \\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nMost issue views in Linear have an Insights panel in the righthand sidebar, which you can open with Cmd/Ctrl Shift I. Find Insights in Custom Views as well as team, project, and cycle views.\\\\n\\\\nExamples\\\\n\\\\nThe best way to learn how Insights works is to try it. We’ve built an in-app Help Center that walks you through common use cases. You can view instructions on how to create these examples or select a button to apply the example to your current view.  \\\\n\\\\nAccess the Help Center by selecting the … icon from the Insights panel and selecting Insights examples\\\\n\\\\nCreate Insights\\\\n\\\\nApply filters\\\\n\\\\nInsights will offer analytics for all the issues in the view you’re currently looking at. Add or remove filters to change which issues are included in the dataset. The following filters can be especially helpful when creating Insights.\\\\n\\\\nTo include Archived issues in your Insights analysis, open the Insights options (to the left of the three dot menu, just above the Segment dropdown) and toggle on Show archived issues. From there you can also filter out issues without a priority assignment.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Insights Parameters\\\\n\\\\nFrom the dropdown, choose the Measure and Slice for your Insight. This will generate a graph and table showing the data, with the Measure along the y-axis and Slice along the x-axis. Segments are optional and use color to slice the data further.\\\\n\\\\nValues for Measure, Slice, and Segment vary depending on what issues are displayed in your view. \\\\n\\\\nGraph Interactions\\\\n\\\\nInsight graphs are interactive, so that you can play with the dataset to learn more or focus on a specific type of issue.\\\\n\\\\nBar graph\\\\n\\\\nHover over each bar to see data and percentile breakdowns.\\\\n\\\\nSelect full bars or segments to temporarily filter your view to only those issues.\\\\n\\\\nHovering or selecting bars or segments will highlight related data in the table below the graph.\\\\n\\\\nScatterplot\\\\n\\\\nHover over the graph to see markers indicating that the data below the line represents 25%, 50%, 75%, and 95% of issues plotted.\\\\n\\\\nSelect percentile markers to zoom in or out along the y-axis.\\\\n\\\\nHover over points to see the issue details including the issue name, ID, and slice and segment values. This will also highlight related data in the table below the graph.\\\\n\\\\nSelect points to go to open the related issue.\\\\n\\\\nBurn-up charts\\\\n\\\\nBurn-up charts, or cumulative flow diagrams show how streams of work have changed over time. Unlike other insights, burn-up charts display your historical issue data so you can analyze and detect patterns across specific time windows.\\\\n\\\\nBy default, burn-up charts show data in monthly increments. Adjust insights settings to plot the data week over week, or to include archived issues.\\\\n\\\\nWhen applicable, select Burn-up under the Slice menu to visualize your data in this style.\\\\n\\\\nTable (below graph)\\\\n\\\\nHover over row or specific cell to see corresponding data in graph\\\\n\\\\nSelect row, column, or cell to temporarily filter issue view to only those issues\\\\n\\\\nShare Insights\\\\n\\\\nSelect the three dot menu on the top right of the Insights panel to share your Insight. \\\\n\\\\nCopy a link, which can be viewed by anyone else in your workspace\\\\n\\\\nExport to CSV, to explore the data outside of Linear\\\\n\\\\nFull Screen view\\\\n\\\\nInsights can be opened in Full Screen view by selecting the expand button (just above the Segment dropdown). This gives you more room to explore the graph and table data.\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"127f0fbc-dfc3-4416-ae34-2c123017f819\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Customer Requests\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/customer-requests\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Bring the voice of the customer into product development.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCustomer Requests allow you to add detailed user feedback and customer attributes to Linear to better align product work with real user needs:\\\\n\\\\nLink customer requests to specific issues or projects in Linear to make feedback accessible to your product team\\\\n\\\\nSee customer pages displaying all requests made by specific customers along with attributes like revenue, size, and tier\\\\n\\\\nView and filter issues or projects by customer name, count, status, tier revenue, and size to identify high impact product work\\\\n\\\\nWhat this video over to see how Customer Requests works:\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nAdmins can enable Customer Requests in Workspace Settings > Customer requests. Once enabled, anyone in your workspace can view customer pages, create and update customers, and add customer requests to issues and projects in Linear or from a supported integration.\\\\n\\\\nWhen an issue is created from integrations with Intercom, Zendesk, Front, or Asks, it will automatically add a request pointing back to the source. Requests can also be added manually and are viewable in their associated issue and the customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nFrom settings, add further customizations. Choose a team to receive new issues created from a request by default (we recommend selecting a team exclusively used to track feedback). You can also customize revenue units and tiers for different plans or products you offer. Revenue, Tier and Size fields will be automatically populated if you use the Intercom integration, or can be filled when creating customers manually.\\\\n\\\\nCustomers\\\\n\\\\nA Customer in Linear represents an organization using your product. Customers have a unique domain, name, logo, and attributes which can be viewed on their page.\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages show all issues and projects with a request associated with that customer. You can group and order this list of issues to see what issues have been marked as important or what issues are in progress. Click on individual issues to open and view their request on that issue in full detail.\\\\n\\\\nFor customers created through Intercom or manually, you'll also see attributes like the customer's revenue, status, tier, and size on the customer page. Individual customer pages can be favorited so that they're easy to access from the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nOpen Customer pages\\\\n\\\\nUse O then Q, then search for the customer's name\\\\n\\\\nOpen the full customer list with G then Q, then search to narrow down results\\\\n\\\\nSearch for the customer in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu\\\\n\\\\nCreate Customers\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests link user feedback to specific issues in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nAdd requests to issues or projects\\\\n\\\\nA customer request is created automatically whenever you create or link to an issue or project from a supported integration. The request will appear both on the issue/project itself as well as on the associated customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nIntegrations\\\\n\\\\nThe following integrations can create issues with customer requests:\\\\n\\\\nMark as important\\\\n\\\\nRequests can be flagged as important to specific customers. For instance, an issue or project may have requests from five different customers but be marked as important by only one of those customers. This binary indicator, shown as a triangle, lets customer-facing teams separate requests that represent a critical need from those expressing a nice-to-have feature.\\\\n\\\\nSynced Attributes\\\\n\\\\nFrom Settings > Customer Requests > Customer attributes data source, you can choose a data source for Linear customer attributes such as Intercom, Zendesk or Front and create mappings between source tool attributes and attributes on Linear Customers. You can also choose None as a data source to allow more control via the API or directly in the interface. Once an external source is configured the company name and domain are automatically synced.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nOwner, Revenue, Size, Status & Tier can be remapped to pull from data in your source tool. These values  are regularly kept up to date as they will be synced with your source.\\\\n\\\\nYou can also choose to enable manual edits for any attributes that are unmapped. Attributes will always be editable via the Linear API regardless of this UI-specific setting. \\\\n\\\\nBuild customer views\\\\n\\\\nAll customer data brought into Linear can be used to create custom views for both issues and projects to surface the impact of your work on your customers. For example, create a view of issues with requests from Enterprise customers (\\\\\\\"Tier\\\\\\\" property from Intercom), where each issue has at least 20 requests. Ordering this view by customer count surfaces the most highly requested issues to the top. Triangle icons indicating requests marked important surface urgent needs.  Consider consulting this view when planning upcoming cycles to ensure what you ship aligns with the needs of the users. \\\\n\\\\nNotifications \\\\n\\\\nYou'll receive Inbox notifications automatically when new requests are added to issues you subscribe to so you're always in the loop. Optionally, configure additional notification styles for new requests here.\\\\n\\\\nSubscribing to custom views is the best option for more targeted alerts. For example, you might wish to stay aware of issues commonly raised by customers who churn. To track that, create a custom view defined by Customer status includes Churned, and ordering the issues by customer revenue. Click the bell icon to be alerted when a new issue is added to the view, or configure notifications to a Slack channel for greater visibility. \\\\nYou can also open a specific customer page (O then Q) and under the bell icon subscribe to get notified when a request is added, marked important, completed or cancelled.\\\\n\\\\nExport Customer Requests\\\\n\\\\nShould you need to export your customer requests from a particular customer, issue, or project, an option exists in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu. Search the command menu and select  Export customer requests as CSV to take that action.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"5a8e0f5e-a81d-4326-bd46-fe3584699c3f\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Assign and delegate issues\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/assigning-issues\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nIssues in Linear are assigned to a single person at a time, giving teams clear ownership and responsibility. Assignment helps teammates triage, track, and prioritize work.\\\\n\\\\nDelegation is a form of assignment used with agents, allowing them to take action on an issue while the assigned teammate maintains ownership.\\\\n\\\\nAssigning issues\\\\n\\\\nYou can assign issues at any point—while creating them, triaging new work, or reviewing and editing existing issues.\\\\n\\\\nTo assign an issue, open the issue and use the assignee field in the properties sidebar to choose a teammate or agent. You can also assign directly from cards in board views and issue list views by clicking the assignee avatar, or press A when viewing or hovering over an issue to open the assignment menu. \\\\n\\\\nTo assign yourself quickly, press I while viewing an issue or when hovering in list view. \\\\n\\\\nYou can also open the command menu (⌘K) and search for \\\\\\\"Assign to...\\\\\\\" to make updates via keyboard. For bulk assignment, use multi-select in list or board views by typing X when hovering over the issue, and right-click to update the assignee from the bulk action bar.\\\\n\\\\nTo remove an assignee, choose \\\\\\\"No assignee\\\\\\\" from the assignment menu.\\\\n\\\\nAssignment permissions\\\\n\\\\nIssues in public teams can be assigned to any workspace member\\\\n\\\\nPrivate team issues can only be assigned to members of the private team\\\\n\\\\nIssues cannot be assigned to guests and inactive users\\\\n\\\\nDelegating to agents\\\\n\\\\nTo delegate work, you can assign an issue to an agent. When you delegate an issue to an agent, you remain the primary assignee and the agent is added as an additional contributor working on your behalf. \\\\n\\\\nYou can change the agent at any time or remove them by selecting \\\\\\\"No agent\\\\\\\" from the assignment menu. \\\\n\\\\nTo delegate an issue to an agent, make sure the agent has access to the team the issue belongs to. Team membership is set when the agent integration is added to a workspace and can be changed by an admin at any time.\\\\n\\\\nManaging assigned issues\\\\n\\\\nUser views\\\\n\\\\nAssigned issues, even those delegated to an agent, appear in your My Issues default view, where you can review all issues you're responsible for across your workspace. This view updates automatically based on assignment changes to track the progress of your assigned and delegated work. \\\\n\\\\nAssigned and delegated issues also appear in any custom views filtered by Assignee or Agent. \\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nHistory\\\\n\\\\nWhen viewing issues, the assignment and delegation history is tracked in its Activity feed, which shows changes over time and who made them.\\\\n\\\\nInbox\\\\n\\\\nYou are automatically subscribed to issues that are assigned to you. You will be notified of any updates to your assigned issues in your Inbox. You can filter Inbox activity by assignment using the \\\\\\\"Notification type\\\\\\\" filter to focus on issues that have been assigned to you. \\\\n\\\\nSearch\\\\n\\\\nYou can filter your searches by assignee or by the agent they've been delegated to through Search to locate relevant issues based on ownership or automation. \\\\n\\\\nInsights\\\\n\\\\nInsights surface trends in how work is distributed across assigned teammates and agents. You can report on issues by assignee or by the agent they’ve been delegated to, helping teams understand ownership patterns and automation coverage.\\\\n\\\\nAutomation\\\\n\\\\nLinear supports automated issue assignment that helps teams route and manage issues with minimal input.\\\\n\\\\nOptionally enable an automation to automatically assign yourself to issues you create. To set up this automation, refer to Preferences. If you choose not to enable this setting, you can still use the Create more button in an issue draft or press ⌘ + Shift + Enter when submitting an issue to quickly create another with the same assignee. \\\\n\\\\nLinear doesn’t currently support auto-assigning issues to a specific teammate by default, but you can use templates to pre-fill the assignee field.\\\\n\\\\nWhen a teammate creates a Git branch from an issue, it can automatically assign the issue to them and move it to a started status when you copy the git branch name. This is configurable in Preferences. \\\\n\\\\nFor custom rules to assign issues when they enter Triage, you can configure triage routing. Based on issue properties like team, status, or label, these rules route issues to a specific team and set an assignee. Rules can also delegate issues to an agent as part of the same flow for even greater automation during triage.\\\\n\\\\nShare issues from private teams\\\\n\\\\nYou can share individual issues from private teams with specific users outside of the team. This is especially useful when bringing collaborators on to solve individual problems for highly sensitive teams, like security or HR. You can assign them a specific issue from your team without giving them access to the rest of the team's data.\\\\n\\\\nShared issues will have a banner prominently displayed to indicate who that issue is visible to.\\\\n\\\\nTo share an issue, choose “Share issue” from the … menu, or hit CMD/CTRL+K and type “Share issue”.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"dcb3e0af-5022-4d0d-9fd0-d566c89b5a25\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Salesforce\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/salesforce\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Create or link to issues from cases. Linear's Salesforce integration is available as an add-on to our Enterprise plan.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nSalesforce users—whether or not they use Linear—can link cases to existing issues and projects or create new issues using a Linear template.\\\\n\\\\nReal-time updates ensure Salesforce users stay informed of issue/project status and priority changes as they happen, streamlining collaboration between teams.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nContact us\\\\n\\\\nPlease reach out to sales@linear.app to purchase licenses for this integration. Once purchased, assign them to Salesforce users to enable them to access the integration.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure from Salesforce App Exchange\\\\n\\\\nInstall the Linear integration from the Salesforce Marketplace\\\\n\\\\nFind the Linear Development app in the Salesforce App Launcher. In order to see this app in the menu launcher, you must have been granted a licence to use this integration. \\\\n\\\\nClick Login with Linear\\\\n\\\\nSelect the Linear workspace you want to connect\\\\n\\\\nConfirm the integration between Salesforce and Linear\\\\n\\\\nConfigure from Linear\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Settings > Integrations > Salesforce\\\\n\\\\nClick Enable\\\\n\\\\nCopy the URL from any page in your Salesforce installation\\\\n\\\\nPaste the URL in the modal in Linear settings\\\\n\\\\nEnable the Linear component\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to a case detail page (commonly in Service Cloud or Support Cloud)\\\\n\\\\nEdit the case detail page\\\\n\\\\nFind Linear in the list of custom components\\\\n\\\\nPlace the component in the location of your choice on the page\\\\n\\\\nLinear x Salesforce integration configuration video\\\\n\\\\nWatch a video overview of how to set up the Salesforce integration.\\\\n\\\\nPermissions\\\\n\\\\nThree permission sets will become available in Salesforce:\\\\n\\\\nSettings\\\\n\\\\nRestrict issue visibility\\\\n\\\\nOnce enabled, only Linear issues that were created from or previously linked to Salesforce can be found when searching from the Linear component. This allows teams with privacy concerns to reduce the scope of visible issues in the Salesforce workspace.\\\\n\\\\nInternal notes\\\\n\\\\nAutomatically notify your team in Salesforce when an issue linked to a case has been completed to cancelled. Changes will be added to the All updates section of a case.\\\\n\\\\nAutomatic case reopening\\\\n\\\\nWhen the linked Linear issue is completed or canceled, you can automate reopening the case to the case status of your choice. This signals your team to follow up with customers.\\\\n\\\\nCase statuses are customizable. If a new case status is added, visiting the Salesforce integration's page in Linear settings will reflect the addition.\\\\n\\\\nTemplates\\\\n\\\\nOnce a template has been created in Linear, click the + icon to make a template available to Salesforce.\\\\n\\\\nMapping attributes from Salesforce to Linear\\\\n\\\\nWhen Customer Requests is enabled, creating issues from a Salesforce case will create new Customers in Linear as needed. The customer request added to the created issue contains the case description.\\\\n\\\\nCustomers support attributes you can use to group, order, and filter for data in Linear. For example, filter issues by the tier of the Customer who requested them, order projects by the associated sum of attributed customer revenue, and more. To bring this data into Linear from Salesforce, set the desired mapping between Salesforce account properties and Customer attributes in Linear in Settings > Customer Requests. \\\\n\\\\nYou can set mappings with any property you use on Salesforce accounts, as long as the data type is allowable:\\\\n\\\\nIf you're using lookup fields in Salesforce and wish to map to these in Linear, consider using formula fields as described in the FAQ.\\\\n\\\\nUsing the integration\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue\\\\n\\\\nFrom a case's details, click Create issue\\\\n\\\\nSelect a template. All issues created by this integration use templates to drive consistency.\\\\n\\\\nInclude a title\\\\n\\\\nWrite a new issue description, or turn on the Include case description toggle to automatically insert the case description into the Linear issue description. The case description will appear in Linear as the Customer Request attached to the issue.\\\\n\\\\nLink cases to existing issues or projects\\\\n\\\\nClick Link issue or project (depending on permission levels)\\\\n\\\\nSearch for the issue or project and confirm\\\\n\\\\nLink to cases from Linear\\\\n\\\\nYou can also link a Linear issue to a case by pasting the case's URL into the Linear issue as a link attachment with Control + L.\\\\n\\\\nSynced details\\\\n\\\\nStatus and priority\\\\n\\\\nWhen an issue is linked to a case, a reference to that issue appears in the Salesforce integration. The linked issue's status and priority are always current.\\\\n\\\\nUser account attribution\\\\n\\\\nIf a Salesforce user is also a Linear user, their name will be displayed as the issue creator in relevant issue activity sections. If the user does not have a Linear account, the issue creator displays as “User User” (the name of the Salesforce developer account) and the email of the Salesforce user who created or linked the issue.\\\\n\\\\nCustomer information\\\\n\\\\nLinear syncs customer information from the Salesforce workspace. Modify attribute mapping as discussed in Mapping attributes above.\\\\n\\\\nTriage rules\\\\n\\\\nUsing the Salesforce integration concurrently with Triage rules offers tools to direct how issues are routed after they're created from Salesforce. You can create rule conditions using properties of the Salesforce Case specifically, along with the normal set of filterable issue data in Linear. Triage rules can set an issue's team, status, assignee, label, project, and priority.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}]\"}]}"}}]}

tasks.prompt: How do I use keyboard shortcuts in Linear?

task_result.final_answer:
Of course! Linear is designed to be keyboard-first, so there are many shortcuts to help you work faster. Here are some of the most common ones:

### General
- **Open Command Menu:** `Cmd/Ctrl` + `K` - This is the most powerful shortcut. You can access almost any action in Linear from here.
- **Create a New Issue:** `C`
- **Create a New Issue (Fullscreen):** `V`
- **Create an Issue from a Template:** `Option/Alt` + `C`
- **Toggle Board/List View:** `Cmd/Ctrl` + `B`
- **Clear Selection:** `Esc`

### Navigating Issues
- **Navigate Up/Down:** `↑` / `↓` or `J` / `K`
- **Go to Active Issues:** `G` then `A`
- **Go to Backlog:** `G` then `B`
- **Go to Archive:** `G` then `X`

### Selecting and Acting on Issues
- **Select a Highlighted Issue:** `X`
- **Select a Range of Issues:** Hold `Shift` while using the up/down arrows.
- **Select All Issues in View:** `Cmd/Ctrl` + `A`
- **Update Multiple Issues:** Select the issues you want to change, then press the key for the property you want to update (e.g., `P` for priority, `A` for assignee).

### Moving Issues
- **Move to Top of a List/Column:** `Option/Alt` + `Shift` + `↑`
- **Move to Bottom of a List/Column:** `Option/Alt` + `Shift` + `↓`

### Issue Relations
- **Add a Related Issue:** `M` then `R`
- **Mark as Blocked By:** `M` then `B`
- **Mark as Blocking:** `M` then `X`
- **Mark as a Duplicate:** `M` then `M`

You can discover more actions and their corresponding shortcuts by opening the **Command Menu** (`Cmd/Ctrl` + `K`) and searching for what you want to do.

tool_calls.name: search_documentation
tool_calls.result: {"content":[{"type":"resource","resource":{"uri":"inline://execute-tool","mimeType":"application/json","text":"{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"2058b036-2564-449e-a1e1-5b6325fc5eba\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Select issues\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/select-issues\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Take actions on groups of issues.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nHighlighting or selecting one issue both allow you to take actions on the issue using keyboard shortcuts, the command menu, or right-click to bring up the contextual menu. The two differentiates when it comes to multiple issues.\\\\n\\\\nBy default, no issue is selected when you open a board or list of issues.\\\\n\\\\nHighlight an issue\\\\n\\\\nHover over the issue with your cursor until the issue is highlighted\\\\n\\\\nUse ↑ / ↓ or J / K to navigate the page to the issue.\\\\n\\\\nSelect issues\\\\n\\\\nOnce an issue is highlighted, press X shortcut.\\\\n\\\\nHold Shift and click your mouse on the issue.\\\\n\\\\nBring up a checkbox element by moving your cursor over the far left of any issue (near where the issue meets the sidebar).\\\\n\\\\nSelect multiple issues\\\\n\\\\nOnce an issue is selected, use the navigation to highlight another issue and press X.\\\\n\\\\nTo select multiple consecutive issues:\\\\n\\\\nHold down Shift after selecting the first issue, then use the ↑ / ↓ keys to increase the selected range one issue at a time.\\\\n\\\\nUse filters to refine the issue list first, then use Cmd/Ctrl A to select all issues on a board or list.\\\\n\\\\nClear selection\\\\n\\\\nPress Esc will clear selected issues on your list or board.\\\\n\\\\nTake actions\\\\n\\\\nOnce an issue or set of issues are selected, use Cmd/Ctrl K to open the command bar and select the preferred action or right-click anywhere on the selected issue(s) to open the contextual menu for a list of available actions.\\\\n\\\\nMoving issues to top, bottom, or in increments\\\\n\\\\nTo move an issue or issues up or down the list:\\\\n\\\\nIn the Display Options, set the Grouping to No grouping.\\\\n\\\\nSet the Ordering to Manual.\\\\n\\\\nSelect the issue(s) you want to move.\\\\n\\\\nUse the shortcut Option/Alt Shift ↑ or Option/Alt Shift ↓ to move an issue/issues in increments.\\\\n\\\\nUpdate multiple issues\\\\n\\\\nSelect them with shortcuts or the mouse and then update the issue field like you would any issue. Common bulk actions will show up at the bottom.\\\\n\\\\nSelect all keyboard shortcut: To change issue priority so all issues without a priority were assigned no priority, filter to no priority issues and then used Cmd/Ctrl A then P to update the priority.\\\\n\\\\nUse mouse to select multiple issues: To move some issues from Todo to backlog, select by hovering over them and individually marking the checkbox that popped up.\\\\n\\\\nUse keyboard to select multiple issues and bulk action toolbar: Holding down Shift while moving the up/down buttons to select multiple issues and choose a bulk action such as  Move to backlog.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"b1eb8e7e-b7d6-49ea-b693-9224b15f3dc1\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Board layout\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/board-layout\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Nearly all views in Linear can be shown in board layout in addition to list view.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nYou can toggle on board layout with the keyboard shortcut Cmd/Ctrl B. Alternatively, you can switch between board or list layout using the board and list icons next to Display options. \\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nFeature parity\\\\n\\\\nFunctionality and keyboard shortcuts are almost exactly the same on board and list views with a few exceptions.\\\\n\\\\nBoard views and List view cannot be ordered independently. If a List view is ordered by priority for example, Board view will be ordered the same way.\\\\n\\\\nBoard views to default to grouping by Status but you can click into Display options and Grouping to group by Focus, Status, Project, Priority, Cycle, Label, Label group, or SLA status (if applicable).\\\\n\\\\nWhen grouping by status, board views are always ordered with statuses from first to last, whereas some list views have unique ordering for statuses.\\\\n\\\\nYou can hide columns in board views but in list views, you can only temporarily hide the equivalent column/grouping option by using filters. You can then make a custom view if you'd like these filters to be permanent.\\\\n\\\\nThe same shortcuts work to select issues in the board view, such as X to select specific issues and Shift X or Shift Click to select multiple. \\\\n\\\\nHide columns\\\\n\\\\nHide columns you don't need by selecting Hide from the column menu (three dots). Hidden columns will show up as the last column on your board. Drag issues into hidden columns without having to unhide them.\\\\n\\\\nMove to top or bottom\\\\n\\\\nTo avoid dragging issues up or down long columns, use keyboard shortcuts instead. Option/Alt Shift Up will move issues to the top of a column whereas Option/Alt Shift Down will move it to the bottom. By default, when you move issues to a new column on a board, it will go to the top if you make the change with the keyboard shortcut S or command menu and to wherever you placed it if you used the mouse.\\\\n\\\\nNavigating columns\\\\n\\\\nBoard views with multiple columns can be scrolled horizontally by holding Shift and scrolling vertically, or using horizontal scroll if your device supports it (like on a trackpad or Magic Mouse). \\\\n\\\\nAlternatively, click and pull an empty space in the board to scroll horizontally.\\\\n\\\\nSwimlanes\\\\n\\\\nBoard and list views have the option to display an extra dimension for visualising your projects or issues in lanes or rows on a board view, commonly known as swimlanes in project management. \\\\n\\\\nThese sub-grouping options are especially useful for a high-level overview of the company's progress or resource allocation. You can sort by columns, group those columns and lay them out as rows for your chosen dimension such as teams, cycles, assignee, lead, initiative or project health to name a few.\\\\n\\\\nThese lanes can be collapsed or expanded for further visual clarity in board view.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"5e2f08d9-b44f-47ef-9fe5-da59a68454c6\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Team pages\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/default-team-pages\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Linear teams contain default views to organize issues and projects. \\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nTeams on your sidebar have the following pages: \\\\n\\\\nTriage (when enabled)\\\\n\\\\nIssues\\\\n\\\\nCycles (when enabled)\\\\n\\\\nCurrent\\\\n\\\\nUpcoming\\\\n\\\\nProjects\\\\n\\\\nViews\\\\n\\\\nArchived issues can be found under the ...  menu when hovering over a Team name or by using the keyboard shortcut g followed by x.\\\\n\\\\nYou can access views from the sidebar or with keyboard shortcuts. \\\\n\\\\nIssues\\\\n\\\\nThe Issues page shows all issues across the selected team that are not archived or deleted. This includes completed and canceled issues. There are default views within this page for Active issues and Backlog issues.\\\\n\\\\nActive\\\\n\\\\nActive shows all active issues on your team, which are the issues under Unstarted or Started categories in your team's workflow. If you're using our default states, these would include To Do and In Progress but not Backlog, Completed, or Canceled issues. Navigate to your team's active issues by hitting G then A.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nBacklog\\\\n\\\\nThis includes issues in backlog, which is our default status for newly created unstarted issues. Navigate to your team's backlog issues by hitting G then B.\\\\n\\\\nOne backlog status exists by default but you can add more statuses in this group through team settings (e.g. Icebox). Any issues here will be updated to active (To do) status if they're moved to a cycle. You can also enable a cycle setting to automate the reverse: if issues are moved out of a cycle, they'll move to the backlog. See cycles under team settings for more information.\\\\n\\\\nArchive\\\\n\\\\nYour team's archive page contains all archived issues, projects, and cycles for the team as well as recently deleted items. Archived items are kept for historical reference and can be restored anytime using the # shortcut. Issue links will still work even if the issue has been archived or is awaiting deletion but issues and projects must be unarchived in order to make changes to them. Deleted items are permanently removed 14 days after deletion.\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to your team's archives page by clicking on the three dots next to your team's name in the sidebar and selecting Open archives... or use the keyboard shortcut G then X.\\\\n\\\\nCycles\\\\n\\\\nThe Cycles page displays current, future, and past (unarchived) cycles and and shows some high level overview for you to compare between cycles. Click into any cycle here to view cycle issues and graphs. Past cycles will be archived according to the settings in auto-archive.\\\\n\\\\nCurrent cycle opens the current cycle and Upcoming opens the next cycle.\\\\n\\\\nProjects\\\\n\\\\nProjects shows all projects what are assigned to the team.\\\\n\\\\nViews\\\\n\\\\nSee existing custom views created for your team or workspace. You can also create new issue or project views from here.\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"103cbd62-42b6-4c06-9ba5-93e766bfb03e\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Create issues\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/creating-issues\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Creating issues is the most common action taken in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nIssues are always linked to a single team. They have an issue ID (team's issue identifier and unique number) and are required to have a title and a status—all other properties and relations are optional.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue\\\\n\\\\nUse the keyboard shortcut C to open up an issue creation modal\\\\n\\\\nUse V to create an issue in full screen mode.\\\\n\\\\nClick the Create new issue icon in the upper left of the app.\\\\n\\\\nTo create an issue from a template use Option/Alt C, or hold Option/Alt and click the Create new issue icon.\\\\n\\\\nEnter https://linear.new into your browser URL bar to create new a issue. It will redirect you to the new issue creation page as long as you are logged into your Linear account.\\\\n\\\\nIssues can be created using our GraphQL API. Many integrations in our directory allow creating issues from other services using this functionality.\\\\n\\\\nIf you have text highlighted when you go to create an issue, this will pre-fill in the issue title.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue via email\\\\n\\\\nIssues can be created by sending an email to a unique intake email address that routes to a team. You can also create an issue via email based off a template.\\\\n\\\\nA link to the original email will be included as an attachment on the Linear issue. Attachments will be synced over, though email attachments are limited to 25 MB.\\\\n\\\\nThe sender of the email will not be notified when the issue is updated or resolved.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an email address\\\\n\\\\nTo create a email address for a Linear team, navigate to Settings > Teams > General > Create by email and enable the toggle.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an email address for a template\\\\n\\\\nTo create an email addresses for a team template:\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Settings > Teams > Select Team > Templates\\\\n\\\\nClick the three dots on the right of the template.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Configure email address.\\\\n\\\\nEnable the toggle in the pop-up.\\\\n\\\\nClick Continue.\\\\n\\\\nWhen a team template is used, the issue's title and description will be overwritten by the email contents, but the properties of the template will be applied to the new issue. Replies sent on the original email to the forwarding address will not create additional issues. \\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues\\\\n\\\\nYou can create recurring issues to automate your repeated tasks on a cadence of your choosing.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a recurring issue\\\\n\\\\nWhen creating a new issue, you can choose to make it into recurring in the issue composer using the …  menu and choosing \\\\\\\"Make recurring…\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\nConvert an existing issue into a recurring issue\\\\n\\\\nTo convert any issue into a recurring issue, open the issue and in the …  menu choose Convert into > Recurring issue…\\\\nYou can also use the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu by typing \\\\\\\"Convert into recurring issue\\\\\\\".\\\\nYou can then choose your first due date, and the cadence at which it repeats.\\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues in team settings\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Team settings > Recurring issues.\\\\nTo create a new recurring issue, click the + icon and set your chosen schedule of recurrence.\\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues from templates\\\\n\\\\nIf you have an existing issue template you want to turn into a recurring issue you can do so. \\\\n\\\\nFirst create your issue and apply your chosen template. \\\\nOnce created, choose the options to convert to a recurring issue from the … menu or command menu. This issue will now turn into a recurring issue, and include any properties that were in the template, including sub-issues.\\\\n\\\\nRecurring issues can easily be found from your Team settings > Recurring issues, or filtering your views using the Recurring issues filter.\\\\n\\\\nCadence of recurring issues\\\\n\\\\nOnce you create a recurring issue, future issues in the cadence are expected to be created once the due date passes (00:01 the following day in your team’s timezone.)\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue URL\\\\n\\\\nThe following links trigger the creation of a new Linear issue in any browser and you can add query parameters after any of them to pre-fill issue fields.\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.app/team/<team ID>/new\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.app/new\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.new\\\\n\\\\nTo pre-fill issue fields and/or properties:\\\\n\\\\nAdd a ? at the end of the link.\\\\n\\\\nInclude the field or issue property you want to pre-set.\\\\n\\\\nAdd =.\\\\n\\\\nAdd the parameter you are setting.\\\\n\\\\nUse & between each field or issue property when creating a string of pre-settings.\\\\n\\\\nApply pre-set properties\\\\n\\\\nWe support the following query parameters:\\\\n\\\\ntitle and description: \\\\n\\\\nUse + to indicate empty space in the keyword, or fully url encode content if more complex as description can be a markdown document.\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?title=My+issue+title&description=This+is+my+issue+description\\\\n\\\\nstatus\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or name of the workflow status\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?status=Todo\\\\n\\\\npriority\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by Urgent, High, Medium or Low\\\\n\\\\nFor example: https://linear.new?priority=Urgent\\\\n\\\\nassignee\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID, display name/name of the user, or assignee=me to assign the creator\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?assignee=john or https://linear.new?assignee=Erin+Baker or https://linear.new?assignee=me.\\\\n\\\\nestimate\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by their point number e.g. estimate=4\\\\n\\\\nT-shirt sizes have the following point values: No priority (0), XS (1), S(2), M (3), L (5), XL (8), XXL (13), XXXL (21)\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?estimate=2\\\\n\\\\ncycle\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID, cycle number of a name of a cycle\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/MOB?cycle=36 or https://linear.new/team/EU/new?cycle=focus+on+bugs\\\\n\\\\nlabel\\\\n\\\\nUse a comma between each label you want to apply\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?label=bug or https://linear.new?labels=bug,android,comments\\\\n\\\\nproject\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or the name of the project\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements\\\\n\\\\nmilestone (a project must be defined)\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or the name of the project milestone\\\\n\\\\nProject milestone can be read only if project is also passed in the URL\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements&projectMilestone=Beta\\\\n\\\\nlinks\\\\n\\\\nURL encoded comma delimited urls with optional title, in format url|title . These will be attached to the issue as link attachments. \\\\n\\\\nFor example: https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?links=https%3A%2F%2Flinear.app%2Chttp%3A%2F%2Fgoogle.com%7CGoogle\\\\n\\\\ntemplate\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID \\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?template=30cd8534-6b24-40bb-8ee7-bb0d83df7d4f\\\\n\\\\nCreate a URL from a template\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Team > Templates.\\\\n\\\\nClick the three dots to the right of the template.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Copy URL to create issue from template.\\\\n\\\\nCopy a URL from an issue\\\\n\\\\nWhile on an issue's view, or an issue is highlighted or selected, open command bar by typing Cmd/Ctrl + K and select Copy pre-filled create issue URL to clipboard.\\\\n\\\\nDrafts\\\\n\\\\nWhen writing an issue and navigating away to another part of the app, Linear will hide the issue modal and keep a temporary draft. The next time you go to create an issue, the editor will re-open with the previous content draft. This type of draft is saved locally and only available on the client used to create it. Logging out, restarting, or resetting Linear will clear this type of draft. \\\\n\\\\nIf you use Esc or click on the close button, a pop-up modal will appear giving you the option to save the issue as a draft. This draft type persists across clients and will not clear on logout or reset. To access your saved drafts, open the Drafts page in your sidebar.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"71a96df4-dcc4-4851-9f2f-627713d0188f\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Issue relations\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/issue-relations\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Create relationships between issues such as blocking, related, and duplicate.\\\\n\\\\nOverview \\\\n\\\\nTag issues with relations and dependencies to help your team identify and remove blockers and work on the most important issues. You can mark issues as blocked, blocking, related, and duplicate.\\\\n\\\\nAdd relationships\\\\n\\\\nThe issue and type of relationship will show up in the issue properties sidebar.\\\\n\\\\nFrom the issue editor, add a relation using keyboard shortcuts or by selecting the overflow menu after saving the issue. From a list or board, use either the keyboard shortcut, command menu or contextual menu. You'll be prompted to find and select the related issue. You can add as many related, blocking, blocked, or duplicate flags as you like but will have to repeat the steps for each one.\\\\n\\\\nRelated issue\\\\n\\\\nWhen you reference issues in a description or comment, they'll automatically become a related issue. Alternatively, use M + R to relate one issue to another. When viewing an issue you can open the command menu (⌘/CTRL+K) and type \\\\\\\"Create new issue related to…\\\\\\\".\\\\n\\\\nTo remove a related issue, hover over the related issue and click the X. You can also select Remove relation from the command menu ⌘/Ctrl K. If you have more than one relation, you'll be prompted to select which relation to remove.\\\\n\\\\nBlocked / blocking\\\\n\\\\nMark issues as blocked by other issues with M then B. If other issues are blocking the current issue, they'll show up in the issue sidebar with an orange flag under Blocked by.\\\\n\\\\nMark issues as blocking other issues with M then X. If the issue is blocking other work, the blocked issues show up in the issue sidebar with a red flag under Blocks.\\\\n\\\\nOnce the blocking issue has been resolved, the blocked issue flag turns green and moves under Related.\\\\n\\\\nDuplicate\\\\n\\\\nMerge duplicate issues into the canonical (saved) issue with the shortcut by pressing M and  M. This will mark the issue you are viewing or have selected as a duplicate issue and change the status to canceled. You cannot mark issues the other way around (e.g. view the canonical issue and mark other issues as duplicates of it). If you have more than one canceled status, you can specify which status to apply under Team Settings > Workflow.\\\"}]\"}]}"}}]}

task_result.final_answer:
Here are the projects in the Linear workspace:

*   [Standardization Groups](https://linear.app/cobrowser/project/standardization-groups-32e6a2bb3544): Standards and community participation for the Q1 plan.
*   [Marketing](https://linear.app/cobrowser/project/marketing-5946aa1f4564): Marketing goals for the Q1 plan.

tasks.prompt: Search Linear documentation for information about integrations.

task_result.final_answer:
I found several documents in the Linear documentation related to integrations. Here is a summary of the top results:

*   **[Integration Directory](https://linear.app/docs/integration-directory)**: This document provides an overview of the Integration Directory, which features apps and add-ons created by the Linear team as well as external applications. You can install these to improve your workflow and sync with your favorite tools. You can also build your own integrations and submit them to the directory.
*   **[Zapier](https://linear.app/docs/zapier)**: This integration lets you build custom automations to create or update Linear issues when actions are taken on other apps or using any of Zapier's triggers.
*   **[Sentry](https://linear.app/docs/sentry)**: Our integration with Sentry lets you create Linear issues from Sentry and create links between Linear and Sentry issues.
*   **[Zendesk](https://linear.app/docs/zendesk)**: Our Zendesk integration makes it easier for you and your team to keep track of bugs and feature requests and interact with the customers who report them.
*   **[Intercom](https://linear.app/docs/intercom)**: Our Intercom integration makes it easy to keep track of bugs and feature requests and interact with the customers who report them.
