https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements\\\\n\\\\nmilestone (a project must be defined)\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or the name of the project milestone\\\\n\\\\nProject milestone can be read only if project is also passed in the URL\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements&projectMilestone=Beta\\\\n\\\\nlinks\\\\n\\\\nURL encoded comma delimited urls with optional title, in format url|title . These will be attached to the issue as link attachments. \\\\n\\\\nFor example: https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?links=https%3A%2F%2Flinear.app%2Chttp%3A%2F%2Fgoogle.com%7CGoogle\\\\n\\\\ntemplate\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID \\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?template=30cd8534-6b24-40bb-8ee7-bb0d83df7d4f\\\\n\\\\nCreate a URL from a template\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Team > Templates.\\\\n\\\\nClick the three dots to the right of the template.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Copy URL to create issue from template.\\\\n\\\\nCopy a URL from an issue\\\\n\\\\nWhile on an issue's view, or an issue is highlighted or selected, open command bar by typing Cmd/Ctrl + K and select Copy pre-filled create issue URL to clipboard.\\\\n\\\\nDrafts\\\\n\\\\nWhen writing an issue and navigating away to another part of the app, Linear will hide the issue modal and keep a temporary draft. The next time you go to create an issue, the editor will re-open with the previous content draft. This type of draft is saved locally and only available on the client used to create it. Logging out, restarting, or resetting Linear will clear this type of draft. \\\\n\\\\nIf you use Esc or click on the close button, a pop-up modal will appear giving you the option to save the issue as a draft. This draft type persists across clients and will not clear on logout or reset. To access your saved drafts, open the Drafts page in your sidebar.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"26e5ac68-6d40-430d-8c11-4828b2993b6a\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Zapier\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/zapier\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"This integration lets you build custom automations to create or update Linear issues when actions are taken on other apps or using any of Zapier's triggers.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nZapier is a good solution for building no-code automations with Linear.\\\\n\\\\nGetting Started\\\\n\\\\nCreate a Zapier account and then create workflows using our Zapier integration. This integration is open source and you're welcome to contribute to it. Zapier has a free trial after which you're charged based on usage.\\\\n\\\\nYou can create workflows with apps such as Typeform, Gmail, Intercom, Google Forms, Discord, Airtable, Todoist, Productboard and more.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nBuild workflows\\\\n\\\\nOur Zapier trigger can be combined with other app triggers or with Zapier's default triggers to build workflows and automations. For instance, you can set up a Zapier workflow that creates a Linear issue whenever a specific Typeform is filled out. \\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nActions\\\\n\\\\nYou can take the following actions in Linear in response to a trigger you configure in Zapier:\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdate an issue\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue attachment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new comment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new project\\\\n\\\\nTriggers\\\\n\\\\nIf Linear is the start of your Zap, the following actions can be used as triggers for downstream actions in Linear or other applications:\\\\n\\\\nNew issue\\\\n\\\\nNew issue comment\\\\n\\\\nNew document comment\\\\n\\\\nNew project\\\\n\\\\nNew project update\\\\n\\\\nNew project update comment\\\\n\\\\nUpdated issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdated project update\\\\n\\\\nIssues created through Zapier appear as created by “Zapier” and not the user who authorized the application.\\\\n\\\\nExample workflows\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue with a bug label when you receive an email message with specific keywords.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue when a tag has been added to an Intercom conversation.\\\\n\\\\nLet team members or clients outside of Linear create bug reports and feature requests via an online form.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue whenever a custom database query returns a new row.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a dealflow pipeline: use a form integration such as Typeform or Google Forms to create a new Linear issue with a custom description.\\\\n\\\\nUpdate to latest version\\\\n\\\\nIf you're not seeing full functionality, you may be running an old version of the Zapier integration. Update the version you're using in a Zap from the status pane:\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"9059eec0-de01-427b-a2bc-26a8deafd38c\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Issue templates\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/issue-templates\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Templates speed up issue creation, ensure properties are applied when necessary, and facilitate reporting.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nTemplates help you file issues more quickly, and ensure desired issue properties are applied without having to add each one manually. When a template is set as default for a team, each new issue in that team will be created from that template unless manually changed.\\\\n\\\\nCreate standard issue templates\\\\n\\\\nCreate new templates by navigating to either Workspace settings > Templates or Team settings > Templates. Standard templates allow you set the properties of the issue, and provide some context in the description if desired.\\\\n\\\\nStandard templates have the same formatting options of regular issues, with the addition of placeholder text. If you want to prompt the creator to input information in the issue's description, consider using this formatting in your template. \\\\n\\\\nTo format text as a placeholder, select text while editing your template and click the Aa icon on the formatting bar. This formatting type is only available when creating or editing templates.\\\\n\\\\nCreate form templates\\\\n\\\\nForm templates are more structured than standard issue templates and can be used when it's important that certain information is provided at issue creation. They support a set of generic form fields that the submitting user can fill out, as well as fillable fields that correspond to the issue's properties directly. Form templates can be triggered in the Slack integration and in Asks, and also directly in Linear by selecting the template. Any field can be required in a form template. \\\\n\\\\nGeneric form fields\\\\n\\\\nProperty form fields\\\\n\\\\nYou can also elect to include customer, a label group, priority, title, and due date. These fields are treated like generic fields as the user fills the form, but they correspond directly to an issue property rather than being a value input into the created issue's description.\\\\n\\\\nDefault properties\\\\n\\\\nLike standard templates, some properties can be applied by default. These appear to the user when the form template is triggered in Linear, but not elsewhere. These fields are team, status, priority, assignee, project, label, and sub-issue.\\\\n\\\\nUse issue templates\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue from a template with the keyboard shortcut Option/Alt C. Alternatively, access templates directly from the issue creation modal by clicking on Template next to the team name.\\\\n\\\\nIf you don't see the template, check that you're creating the issue from the right team. In a sub-issue, only templates that do not themselves contain sub-issues are available.\\\\n\\\\nDefault templates\\\\n\\\\nDefault templates are templates that are automatically applied when creating a new issue, given the conditions are met. Configure default templates from a team's template settings page. You can configure defaults differently for members of your team or people who are not part of your team. Form templates are only available as default templates for non-team members.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTemplates in integrations\\\\n\\\\nTemplates created in Linear can also be used in integrations to help save time and keep properties applied consistently where needed. \\\\n\\\\nAdd a template to a supported integration in that integration's settings page in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nTemplate based Insights\\\\n\\\\nIssues created by template are filterable by their template, regardless of where they were created. For example, for a template Bug Report, filtering for that template will return issues created with that template in created in Slack with our Slack or Asks integrations, Intercom/Zendesk, and Linear's interface. Questions like \\\\\\\"What's our breakdown of bug reports vs. feature requests look like, and how many of them are solved\\\\\\\" can be explored with Insights:\\\\n\\\\nOr, investigate the breakdown of template use by intake source:\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"d4a4f7b8-87ab-4afd-8b49-a6ec4a8e62b9\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Slack\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/slack\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Combine Linear with Slack to keep everyone in sync.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from Slack messages, sync threads between Slack and Linear, set up personal and channel-specific notifications, display rich unfurls in Slack and more.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nIntegration setup\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Features > Integrations > Slack to connect your Slack account to Linear. You must be a Linear admin to do this. \\\\n\\\\nOnce this has been completed, others in your Linear workspace can:\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear agent by mentioning @Linear\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from the More actions menu on Slack messages or by using the /linear command on all plans\\\\n\\\\nTake quick actions from Linear links shared in your Slack workspace\\\\n\\\\nView rich unfurls in Slack that show key issue, comment, document, initiative or project details\\\\n\\\\nEnable personal Slack notifications\\\\n\\\\nSend team and project updates to dedicated Slack channels\\\\n\\\\nNotifications setup\\\\n\\\\nSlack notifications are available for teams, projects, and individuals. Once the initial integration has been configured, any user can set up these notifications. \\\\n\\\\nConnect multiple Slack workspaces\\\\n\\\\nLinear's Enterprise plan supports connecting multiple Slack workspaces to Linear to use the Slack integration. If you're using Slack's Enterprise Grid plan for example, this would allow you to use the Slack integration across your workspaces. To add a new workspace, go to Linear's Slack integration settings and click the + button under connected workspaces. You must hold Admin permissions on an Enterprise Linear plan in order to enable this feature.\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent for Slack\\\\n\\\\nMention @linear in discussions on Slack, and the Linear agent will create issues informed by your conversation's context. Use natural language to specify issue details or simply let the agent infer what's needed.\\\\n\\\\nFor example, try:\\\\n\\\\n@linear file a bug, assign me\\\\n\\\\n@linear make feature requests from this thread\\\\n\\\\n@linear file to the Rideshare Loyalty project\\\\n\\\\nTo use this functionality, send /invite @linear to the desired Slack channel before sending. This feature is available to users in your Slack workspace with Linear accounts.\\\\n\\\\nIn settings, you can choose to allow Slack’s workflow builder to mention @Linear agent to support automating actions.\\\\n\\\\nSet Linear agent guidance\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent considers instructions you write in Slack integration settings on how to create issues. Use this field to refine the agent's behavior and give it more context about how you use Slack. \\\\n\\\\nYou might give it context about your channel naming structure and how it relates to your Linear projects, what statuses you prefer it create in, the team it should use when unsure, and more.\\\\n\\\\nOutside of this field, the agent also uses contextual clues to help infer where to create issues (for example, if you're sending project updates from a project to a channel that sounds related, issues created in that channel will favor creation in that project.)\\\\n\\\\nCreate issues with message actions\\\\n\\\\nYou can also create issues using the More actions menu on a Slack message if you prefer to specify all the details of your created issue.\\\\n\\\\nIf you select a team in the resulting window that uses default templates, that template's text will appear in the description field. Only Linear users in your Slack workspace can create issues with this integration. \\\\n\\\\nUse templates\\\\n\\\\nYour issue templates in Linear can also be used in Slack. Add templates to your Slack integration from workspace template settings or the Slack settings page. Admins can make up to 10 issue templates available in your Slack integration, which any Linear members in your Slack workspace can view and apply during issue creation. If you have a default template set for your team, it’ll show up as an additional template option after the team has been selected.\\\\n\\\\nTemplates in private teams are not available to the Slack integration (nor in other integrations that support templates like Intercom and Zendesk.) If you need to allow users to create issues using templates in private teams, consider using Asks where this use-case is supported.\\\\n\\\\nSync threads\\\\n\\\\nTo create a synced thread, create an issue from Slack through the More actions menu on a Slack message. \\\\n\\\\nWhen you click Submit, you'll also create a synced comment thread in the Linear issue by default. Both threads will update as replies are sent in either location, and we'll also update the synced thread in Slack when the issue is completed or canceled, or reopened after being completed or canceled.\\\\n\\\\nWhen people in your company report issues in Slack, syncing threads is a great way to keep them in the loop regardless of whether they're in your Linear workspace. Comments made in the synced Linear thread will also appear in Slack, and the Slack thread will be updated when the issue is completed or canceled. \\\\n\\\\nIf an issue synced to a Slack thread is marked as a duplicate of another issue, we'll also update the Slack thread where the duplicate was created once the canonical issue is resolved.\\\\n\\\\nAdd Slack messages to existing issues\\\\n\\\\nThere are a few options to link Slack messages to existing Linear issues.\\\\n\\\\nNotifications functionality\\\\n\\\\nRich unfurls and issue actions \\\\n\\\\nOnce you've connected the integration for Slack, we'll show expanded links anytime you post issue, project, document, or initiative links from public teams in Slack.\\\\n\\\\n URLs associated with private Linear teams never unfurl. Unfurling can be disabled in Settings > Integrations > Slack if desired.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"127f0fbc-dfc3-4416-ae34-2c123017f819\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Customer Requests\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/customer-requests\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Bring the voice of the customer into product development.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCustomer Requests allow you to add detailed user feedback and customer attributes to Linear to better align product work with real user needs:\\\\n\\\\nLink customer requests to specific issues or projects in Linear to make feedback accessible to your product team\\\\n\\\\nSee customer pages displaying all requests made by specific customers along with attributes like revenue, size, and tier\\\\n\\\\nView and filter issues or projects by customer name, count, status, tier revenue, and size to identify high impact product work\\\\n\\\\nWhat this video over to see how Customer Requests works:\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nAdmins can enable Customer Requests in Workspace Settings > Customer requests. Once enabled, anyone in your workspace can view customer pages, create and update customers, and add customer requests to issues and projects in Linear or from a supported integration.\\\\n\\\\nWhen an issue is created from integrations with Intercom, Zendesk, Front, or Asks, it will automatically add a request pointing back to the source. Requests can also be added manually and are viewable in their associated issue and the customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nFrom settings, add further customizations. Choose a team to receive new issues created from a request by default (we recommend selecting a team exclusively used to track feedback). You can also customize revenue units and tiers for different plans or products you offer. Revenue, Tier and Size fields will be automatically populated if you use the Intercom integration, or can be filled when creating customers manually.\\\\n\\\\nCustomers\\\\n\\\\nA Customer in Linear represents an organization using your product. Customers have a unique domain, name, logo, and attributes which can be viewed on their page.\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages show all issues and projects with a request associated with that customer. You can group and order this list of issues to see what issues have been marked as important or what issues are in progress. Click on individual issues to open and view their request on that issue in full detail.\\\\n\\\\nFor customers created through Intercom or manually, you'll also see attributes like the customer's revenue, status, tier, and size on the customer page. Individual customer pages can be favorited so that they're easy to access from the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nOpen Customer pages\\\\n\\\\nUse O then Q, then search for the customer's name\\\\n\\\\nOpen the full customer list with G then Q, then search to narrow down results\\\\n\\\\nSearch for the customer in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu\\\\n\\\\nCreate Customers\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests link user feedback to specific issues in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nAdd requests to issues or projects\\\\n\\\\nA customer request is created automatically whenever you create or link to an issue or project from a supported integration. The request will appear both on the issue/project itself as well as on the associated customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nIntegrations\\\\n\\\\nThe following integrations can create issues with customer requests:\\\\n\\\\nMark as important\\\\n\\\\nRequests can be flagged as important to specific customers. For instance, an issue or project may have requests from five different customers but be marked as important by only one of those customers. This binary indicator, shown as a triangle, lets customer-facing teams separate requests that represent a critical need from those expressing a nice-to-have feature.\\\\n\\\\nSynced Attributes\\\\n\\\\nFrom Settings > Customer Requests > Customer attributes data source, you can choose a data source for Linear customer attributes such as Intercom, Zendesk or Front and create mappings between source tool attributes and attributes on Linear Customers. You can also choose None as a data source to allow more control via the API or directly in the interface. Once an external source is configured the company name and domain are automatically synced.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nOwner, Revenue, Size, Status & Tier can be remapped to pull from data in your source tool. These values  are regularly kept up to date as they will be synced with your source.\\\\n\\\\nYou can also choose to enable manual edits for any attributes that are unmapped. Attributes will always be editable via the Linear API regardless of this UI-specific setting. \\\\n\\\\nBuild customer views\\\\n\\\\nAll customer data brought into Linear can be used to create custom views for both issues and projects to surface the impact of your work on your customers. For example, create a view of issues with requests from Enterprise customers (\\\\\\\"Tier\\\\\\\" property from Intercom), where each issue has at least 20 requests. Ordering this view by customer count surfaces the most highly requested issues to the top. Triangle icons indicating requests marked important surface urgent needs.  Consider consulting this view when planning upcoming cycles to ensure what you ship aligns with the needs of the users. \\\\n\\\\nNotifications \\\\n\\\\nYou'll receive Inbox notifications automatically when new requests are added to issues you subscribe to so you're always in the loop. Optionally, configure additional notification styles for new requests here.\\\\n\\\\nSubscribing to custom views is the best option for more targeted alerts. For example, you might wish to stay aware of issues commonly raised by customers who churn. To track that, create a custom view defined by Customer status includes Churned, and ordering the issues by customer revenue. Click the bell icon to be alerted when a new issue is added to the view, or configure notifications to a Slack channel for greater visibility. \\\\nYou can also open a specific customer page (O then Q) and under the bell icon subscribe to get notified when a request is added, marked important, completed or cancelled.\\\\n\\\\nExport Customer Requests\\\\n\\\\nShould you need to export your customer requests from a particular customer, issue, or project, an option exists in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu. Search the command menu and select  Export customer requests as CSV to take that action.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}]\"}]},\"logs\":[\"[log] {\\n  \\\"content\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"type\\\": \\\"text\\\",\\n      \\\"text\\\": \\\"[{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"103cbd62-42b6-4c06-9ba5-93e766bfb03e\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Create issues\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"url\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"https://linear.app/docs/creating-issues\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Creating issues is the most common action taken in Linear.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOverview\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIssues are always linked to a single team. They have an issue ID (team's issue identifier and unique number) and are required to have a title and a status—all other properties and relations are optional.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate an issue\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUse the keyboard shortcut C to open up an issue creation modal\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUse V to create an issue in full screen mode.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nClick the Create new issue icon in the upper left of the app.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTo create an issue from a template use Option/Alt C, or hold Option/Alt and click the Create new issue icon.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEnter https://linear.new into your browser URL bar to create new a issue. It will redirect you to the new issue creation page as long as you are logged into your Linear account.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIssues can be created using our GraphQL API. Many integrations in our directory allow creating issues from other services using this functionality.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you have text highlighted when you go to create an issue, this will pre-fill in the issue title.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate an issue via email\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIssues can be created by sending an email to a unique intake email address that routes to a team. You can also create an issue via email based off a template.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nA link to the original email will be included as an attachment on the Linear issue. Attachments will be synced over, though email attachments are limited to 25 MB.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe sender of the email will not be notified when the issue is updated or resolved.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate an email address\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTo create a email address for a Linear team, navigate to Settings > Teams > General > Create by email and enable the toggle.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCreate an email address for a template\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTo create an email addresses for a team template:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNavigate to Settings > Teams >...[truncated]\"],\"toolCalls\":[{\"name\":\"EXECUTE_TOOL\",\"args\":{\"tool_id\":\"a7bf0a61-f018-4f75-9375-3e62a100f3df:search_documentation\",\"tool_arguments\":{\"query\":\"create an issue\"}},\"durationMs\":530,\"result\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"103cbd62-42b6-4c06-9ba5-93e766bfb03e\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Create issues\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/creating-issues\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Creating issues is the most common action taken in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nIssues are always linked to a single team. They have an issue ID (team's issue identifier and unique number) and are required to have a title and a status—all other properties and relations are optional.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue\\\\n\\\\nUse the keyboard shortcut C to open up an issue creation modal\\\\n\\\\nUse V to create an issue in full screen mode.\\\\n\\\\nClick the Create new issue icon in the upper left of the app.\\\\n\\\\nTo create an issue from a template use Option/Alt C, or hold Option/Alt and click the Create new issue icon.\\\\n\\\\nEnter https://linear.new into your browser URL bar to create new a issue. It will redirect you to the new issue creation page as long as you are logged into your Linear account.\\\\n\\\\nIssues can be created using our GraphQL API. Many integrations in our directory allow creating issues from other services using this functionality.\\\\n\\\\nIf you have text highlighted when you go to create an issue, this will pre-fill in the issue title.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue via email\\\\n\\\\nIssues can be created by sending an email to a unique intake email address that routes to a team. You can also create an issue via email based off a template.\\\\n\\\\nA link to the original email will be included as an attachment on the Linear issue. Attachments will be synced over, though email attachments are limited to 25 MB.\\\\n\\\\nThe sender of the email will not be notified when the issue is updated or resolved.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an email address\\\\n\\\\nTo create a email address for a Linear team, navigate to Settings > Teams > General > Create by email and enable the toggle.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an email address for a template\\\\n\\\\nTo create an email addresses for a team template:\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Settings > Teams > Select Team > Templates\\\\n\\\\nClick the three dots on the right of the template.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Configure email address.\\\\n\\\\nEnable the toggle in the pop-up.\\\\n\\\\nClick Continue.\\\\n\\\\nWhen a team template is used, the issue's title and description will be overwritten by the email contents, but the properties of the template will be applied to the new issue. Replies sent on the original email to the forwarding address will not create additional issues. \\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues\\\\n\\\\nYou can create recurring issues to automate your repeated tasks on a cadence of your choosing.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a recurring issue\\\\n\\\\nWhen creating a new issue, you can choose to make it into recurring in the issue composer using the …  menu and choosing \\\\\\\"Make recurring…\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\nConvert an existing issue into a recurring issue\\\\n\\\\nTo convert any issue into a recurring issue, open the issue and in the …  menu choose Convert into > Recurring issue…\\\\nYou can also use the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu by typing \\\\\\\"Convert into recurring issue\\\\\\\".\\\\nYou can then choose your first due date, and the cadence at which it repeats.\\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues in team settings\\\\n\\\\nNavigate to Team settings > Recurring issues.\\\\nTo create a new recurring issue, click the + icon and set your chosen schedule of recurrence.\\\\n\\\\nCreate recurring issues from templates\\\\n\\\\nIf you have an existing issue template you want to turn into a recurring issue you can do so. \\\\n\\\\nFirst create your issue and apply your chosen template. \\\\nOnce created, choose the options to convert to a recurring issue from the … menu or command menu. This issue will now turn into a recurring issue, and include any properties that were in the template, including sub-issues.\\\\n\\\\nRecurring issues can easily be found from your Team settings > Recurring issues, or filtering your views using the Recurring issues filter.\\\\n\\\\nCadence of recurring issues\\\\n\\\\nOnce you create a recurring issue, future issues in the cadence are expected to be created once the due date passes (00:01 the following day in your team’s timezone.)\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue URL\\\\n\\\\nThe following links trigger the creation of a new Linear issue in any browser and you can add query parameters after any of them to pre-fill issue fields.\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.app/team/<team ID>/new\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.app/new\\\\n\\\\nhttp://linear.new\\\\n\\\\nTo pre-fill issue fields and/or properties:\\\\n\\\\nAdd a ? at the end of the link.\\\\n\\\\nInclude the field or issue property you want to pre-set.\\\\n\\\\nAdd =.\\\\n\\\\nAdd the parameter you are setting.\\\\n\\\\nUse & between each field or issue property when creating a string of pre-settings.\\\\n\\\\nApply pre-set properties\\\\n\\\\nWe support the following query parameters:\\\\n\\\\ntitle and description: \\\\n\\\\nUse + to indicate empty space in the keyword, or fully url encode content if more complex as description can be a markdown document.\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?title=My+issue+title&description=This+is+my+issue+description\\\\n\\\\nstatus\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or name of the workflow status\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?status=Todo\\\\n\\\\npriority\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by Urgent, High, Medium or Low\\\\n\\\\nFor example: https://linear.new?priority=Urgent\\\\n\\\\nassignee\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID, display name/name of the user, or assignee=me to assign the creator\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new?assignee=john or https://linear.new?assignee=Erin+Baker or https://linear.new?assignee=me.\\\\n\\\\nestimate\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by their point number e.g. estimate=4\\\\n\\\\nT-shirt sizes have the following point values: No priority (0), XS (1), S(2), M (3), L (5), XL (8), XXL (13), XXXL (21)\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?estimate=2\\\\n\\\\ncycle\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID, cycle number of a name of a cycle\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/MOB?cycle=36 or https://linear.new/team/EU/new?cycle=focus+on+bugs\\\\n\\\\nlabel\\\\n\\\\nUse a comma between each label you want to apply\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?label=bug or https://linear.new?labels=bug,android,comments\\\\n\\\\nproject\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or the name of the project\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements\\\\n\\\\nmilestone (a project must be defined)\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID or the name of the project milestone\\\\n\\\\nProject milestone can be read only if project is also passed in the URL\\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?project=Project+page+improvements&projectMilestone=Beta\\\\n\\\\nlinks\\\\n\\\\nURL encoded comma delimited urls with optional title, in format url|title . These will be attached to the issue as link attachments. \\\\n\\\\nFor example: https://linear.new/team/LIN/new?links=https%3A%2F%2Flinear.app%2Chttp%3A%2F%2Fgoogle.com%7CGoogle\\\\n\\\\ntemplate\\\\n\\\\nCan be set by UUID \\\\n\\\\nFor example, https://linear.app/team/LIN/new?template=30cd8534-6b24-40bb-8ee7-bb0d83df7d4f\\\\n\\\\nCreate a URL from a template\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Team > Templates.\\\\n\\\\nClick the three dots to the right of the template.\\\\n\\\\nSelect Copy URL to create issue from template.\\\\n\\\\nCopy a URL from an issue\\\\n\\\\nWhile on an issue's view, or an issue is highlighted or selected, open command bar by typing Cmd/Ctrl + K and select Copy pre-filled create issue URL to clipboard.\\\\n\\\\nDrafts\\\\n\\\\nWhen writing an issue and navigating away to another part of the app, Linear will hide the issue modal and keep a temporary draft. The next time you go to create an issue, the editor will re-open with the previous content draft. This type of draft is saved locally and only available on the client used to create it. Logging out, restarting, or resetting Linear will clear this type of draft. \\\\n\\\\nIf you use Esc or click on the close button, a pop-up modal will appear giving you the option to save the issue as a draft. This draft type persists across clients and will not clear on logout or reset. To access your saved drafts, open the Drafts page in your sidebar.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"26e5ac68-6d40-430d-8c11-4828b2993b6a\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Zapier\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/zapier\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"This integration lets you build custom automations to create or update Linear issues when actions are taken on other apps or using any of Zapier's triggers.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nZapier is a good solution for building no-code automations with Linear.\\\\n\\\\nGetting Started\\\\n\\\\nCreate a Zapier account and then create workflows using our Zapier integration. This integration is open source and you're welcome to contribute to it. Zapier has a free trial after which you're charged based on usage.\\\\n\\\\nYou can create workflows with apps such as Typeform, Gmail, Intercom, Google Forms, Discord, Airtable, Todoist, Productboard and more.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nBuild workflows\\\\n\\\\nOur Zapier trigger can be combined with other app triggers or with Zapier's default triggers to build workflows and automations. For instance, you can set up a Zapier workflow that creates a Linear issue whenever a specific Typeform is filled out. \\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nActions\\\\n\\\\nYou can take the following actions in Linear in response to a trigger you configure in Zapier:\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdate an issue\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue attachment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new comment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new project\\\\n\\\\nTriggers\\\\n\\\\nIf Linear is the start of your Zap, the following actions can be used as triggers for downstream actions in Linear or other applications:\\\\n\\\\nNew issue\\\\n\\\\nNew issue comment\\\\n\\\\nNew document comment\\\\n\\\\nNew project\\\\n\\\\nNew project update\\\\n\\\\nNew project update comment\\\\n\\\\nUpdated issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdated project update\\\\n\\\\nIssues created through Zapier appear as created by “Zapier” and not the user who authorized the application.\\\\n\\\\nExample workflows\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue with a bug label when you receive an email message with specific keywords.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue when a tag has been added to an Intercom conversation.\\\\n\\\\nLet team members or clients outside of Linear create bug reports and feature requests via an online form.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue whenever a custom database query returns a new row.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a dealflow pipeline: use a form integration such as Typeform or Google Forms to create a new Linear issue with a custom description.\\\\n\\\\nUpdate to latest version\\\\n\\\\nIf you're not seeing full functionality, you may be running an old version of the Zapier integration. Update the version you're using in a Zap from the status pane:\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"9059eec0-de01-427b-a2bc-26a8deafd38c\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Issue templates\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/issue-templates\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Templates speed up issue creation, ensure properties are applied when necessary, and facilitate reporting.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nTemplates help you file issues more quickly, and ensure desired issue properties are applied without having to add each one manually. When a template is set as default for a team, each new issue in that team will be created from that template unless manually changed.\\\\n\\\\nCreate standard issue templates\\\\n\\\\nCreate new templates by navigating to either Workspace settings > Templates or Team settings > Templates. Standard templates allow you set the properties of the issue, and provide some context in the description if desired.\\\\n\\\\nStandard templates have the same formatting options of regular issues, with the addition of placeholder text. If you want to prompt the creator to input information in the issue's description, consider using this formatting in your template. \\\\n\\\\nTo format text as a placeholder, select text while editing your template and click the Aa icon on the formatting bar. This formatting type is only available when creating or editing templates.\\\\n\\\\nCreate form templates\\\\n\\\\nForm templates are more structured than standard issue templates and can be used when it's important that certain information is provided at issue creation. They support a set of generic form fields that the submitting user can fill out, as well as fillable fields that correspond to the issue's properties directly. Form templates can be triggered in the Slack integration and in Asks, and also directly in Linear by selecting the template. Any field can be required in a form template. \\\\n\\\\nGeneric form fields\\\\n\\\\nProperty form fields\\\\n\\\\nYou can also elect to include customer, a label group, priority, title, and due date. These fields are treated like generic fields as the user fills the form, but they correspond directly to an issue property rather than being a value input into the created issue's description.\\\\n\\\\nDefault properties\\\\n\\\\nLike standard templates, some properties can be applied by default. These appear to the user when the form template is triggered in Linear, but not elsewhere. These fields are team, status, priority, assignee, project, label, and sub-issue.\\\\n\\\\nUse issue templates\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue from a template with the keyboard shortcut Option/Alt C. Alternatively, access templates directly from the issue creation modal by clicking on Template next to the team name.\\\\n\\\\nIf you don't see the template, check that you're creating the issue from the right team. In a sub-issue, only templates that do not themselves contain sub-issues are available.\\\\n\\\\nDefault templates\\\\n\\\\nDefault templates are templates that are automatically applied when creating a new issue, given the conditions are met. Configure default templates from a team's template settings page. You can configure defaults differently for members of your team or people who are not part of your team. Form templates are only available as default templates for non-team members.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTemplates in integrations\\\\n\\\\nTemplates created in Linear can also be used in integrations to help save time and keep properties applied consistently where needed. \\\\n\\\\nAdd a template to a supported integration in that integration's settings page in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nTemplate based Insights\\\\n\\\\nIssues created by template are filterable by their template, regardless of where they were created. For example, for a template Bug Report, filtering for that template will return issues created with that template in created in Slack with our Slack or Asks integrations, Intercom/Zendesk, and Linear's interface. Questions like \\\\\\\"What's our breakdown of bug reports vs. feature requests look like, and how many of them are solved\\\\\\\" can be explored with Insights:\\\\n\\\\nOr, investigate the breakdown of template use by intake source:\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"d4a4f7b8-87ab-4afd-8b49-a6ec4a8e62b9\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Slack\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/slack\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Combine Linear with Slack to keep everyone in sync.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from Slack messages, sync threads between Slack and Linear, set up personal and channel-specific notifications, display rich unfurls in Slack and more.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nIntegration setup\\\\n\\\\nGo to Settings > Features > Integrations > Slack to connect your Slack account to Linear. You must be a Linear admin to do this. \\\\n\\\\nOnce this has been completed, others in your Linear workspace can:\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear agent by mentioning @Linear\\\\n\\\\nCreate Linear issues from the More actions menu on Slack messages or by using the /linear command on all plans\\\\n\\\\nTake quick actions from Linear links shared in your Slack workspace\\\\n\\\\nView rich unfurls in Slack that show key issue, comment, document, initiative or project details\\\\n\\\\nEnable personal Slack notifications\\\\n\\\\nSend team and project updates to dedicated Slack channels\\\\n\\\\nNotifications setup\\\\n\\\\nSlack notifications are available for teams, projects, and individuals. Once the initial integration has been configured, any user can set up these notifications. \\\\n\\\\nConnect multiple Slack workspaces\\\\n\\\\nLinear's Enterprise plan supports connecting multiple Slack workspaces to Linear to use the Slack integration. If you're using Slack's Enterprise Grid plan for example, this would allow you to use the Slack integration across your workspaces. To add a new workspace, go to Linear's Slack integration settings and click the + button under connected workspaces. You must hold Admin permissions on an Enterprise Linear plan in order to enable this feature.\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent for Slack\\\\n\\\\nMention @linear in discussions on Slack, and the Linear agent will create issues informed by your conversation's context. Use natural language to specify issue details or simply let the agent infer what's needed.\\\\n\\\\nFor example, try:\\\\n\\\\n@linear file a bug, assign me\\\\n\\\\n@linear make feature requests from this thread\\\\n\\\\n@linear file to the Rideshare Loyalty project\\\\n\\\\nTo use this functionality, send /invite @linear to the desired Slack channel before sending. This feature is available to users in your Slack workspace with Linear accounts.\\\\n\\\\nIn settings, you can choose to allow Slack’s workflow builder to mention @Linear agent to support automating actions.\\\\n\\\\nSet Linear agent guidance\\\\n\\\\nLinear agent considers instructions you write in Slack integration settings on how to create issues. Use this field to refine the agent's behavior and give it more context about how you use Slack. \\\\n\\\\nYou might give it context about your channel naming structure and how it relates to your Linear projects, what statuses you prefer it create in, the team it should use when unsure, and more.\\\\n\\\\nOutside of this field, the agent also uses contextual clues to help infer where to create issues (for example, if you're sending project updates from a project to a channel that sounds related, issues created in that channel will favor creation in that project.)\\\\n\\\\nCreate issues with message actions\\\\n\\\\nYou can also create issues using the More actions menu on a Slack message if you prefer to specify all the details of your created issue.\\\\n\\\\nIf you select a team in the resulting window that uses default templates, that template's text will appear in the description field. Only Linear users in your Slack workspace can create issues with this integration. \\\\n\\\\nUse templates\\\\n\\\\nYour issue templates in Linear can also be used in Slack. Add templates to your Slack integration from workspace template settings or the Slack settings page. Admins can make up to 10 issue templates available in your Slack integration, which any Linear members in your Slack workspace can view and apply during issue creation. If you have a default template set for your team, it’ll show up as an additional template option after the team has been selected.\\\\n\\\\nTemplates in private teams are not available to the Slack integration (nor in other integrations that support templates like Intercom and Zendesk.) If you need to allow users to create issues using templates in private teams, consider using Asks where this use-case is supported.\\\\n\\\\nSync threads\\\\n\\\\nTo create a synced thread, create an issue from Slack through the More actions menu on a Slack message. \\\\n\\\\nWhen you click Submit, you'll also create a synced comment thread in the Linear issue by default. Both threads will update as replies are sent in either location, and we'll also update the synced thread in Slack when the issue is completed or canceled, or reopened after being completed or canceled.\\\\n\\\\nWhen people in your company report issues in Slack, syncing threads is a great way to keep them in the loop regardless of whether they're in your Linear workspace. Comments made in the synced Linear thread will also appear in Slack, and the Slack thread will be updated when the issue is completed or canceled. \\\\n\\\\nIf an issue synced to a Slack thread is marked as a duplicate of another issue, we'll also update the Slack thread where the duplicate was created once the canonical issue is resolved.\\\\n\\\\nAdd Slack messages to existing issues\\\\n\\\\nThere are a few options to link Slack messages to existing Linear issues.\\\\n\\\\nNotifications functionality\\\\n\\\\nRich unfurls and issue actions \\\\n\\\\nOnce you've connected the integration for Slack, we'll show expanded links anytime you post issue, project, document, or initiative links from public teams in Slack.\\\\n\\\\n URLs associated with private Linear teams never unfurl. Unfurling can be disabled in Settings > Integrations > Slack if desired.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"127f0fbc-dfc3-4416-ae34-2c123017f819\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Customer Requests\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/customer-requests\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Bring the voice of the customer into product development.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCustomer Requests allow you to add detailed user feedback and customer attributes to Linear to better align product work with real user needs:\\\\n\\\\nLink customer requests to specific issues or projects in Linear to make feedback accessible to your product team\\\\n\\\\nSee customer pages displaying all requests made by specific customers along with attributes like revenue, size, and tier\\\\n\\\\nView and filter issues or projects by customer name, count, status, tier revenue, and size to identify high impact product work\\\\n\\\\nWhat this video over to see how Customer Requests works:\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nAdmins can enable Customer Requests in Workspace Settings > Customer requests. Once enabled, anyone in your workspace can view customer pages, create and update customers, and add customer requests to issues and projects in Linear or from a supported integration.\\\\n\\\\nWhen an issue is created from integrations with Intercom, Zendesk, Front, or Asks, it will automatically add a request pointing back to the source. Requests can also be added manually and are viewable in their associated issue and the customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nFrom settings, add further customizations. Choose a team to receive new issues created from a request by default (we recommend selecting a team exclusively used to track feedback). You can also customize revenue units and tiers for different plans or products you offer. Revenue, Tier and Size fields will be automatically populated if you use the Intercom integration, or can be filled when creating customers manually.\\\\n\\\\nCustomers\\\\n\\\\nA Customer in Linear represents an organization using your product. Customers have a unique domain, name, logo, and attributes which can be viewed on their page.\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages\\\\n\\\\nCustomer pages show all issues and projects with a request associated with that customer. You can group and order this list of issues to see what issues have been marked as important or what issues are in progress. Click on individual issues to open and view their request on that issue in full detail.\\\\n\\\\nFor customers created through Intercom or manually, you'll also see attributes like the customer's revenue, status, tier, and size on the customer page. Individual customer pages can be favorited so that they're easy to access from the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nOpen Customer pages\\\\n\\\\nUse O then Q, then search for the customer's name\\\\n\\\\nOpen the full customer list with G then Q, then search to narrow down results\\\\n\\\\nSearch for the customer in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu\\\\n\\\\nCreate Customers\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests\\\\n\\\\nCustomer requests link user feedback to specific issues in Linear.\\\\n\\\\nAdd requests to issues or projects\\\\n\\\\nA customer request is created automatically whenever you create or link to an issue or project from a supported integration. The request will appear both on the issue/project itself as well as on the associated customer's page.\\\\n\\\\nIntegrations\\\\n\\\\nThe following integrations can create issues with customer requests:\\\\n\\\\nMark as important\\\\n\\\\nRequests can be flagged as important to specific customers. For instance, an issue or project may have requests from five different customers but be marked as important by only one of those customers. This binary indicator, shown as a triangle, lets customer-facing teams separate requests that represent a critical need from those expressing a nice-to-have feature.\\\\n\\\\nSynced Attributes\\\\n\\\\nFrom Settings > Customer Requests > Customer attributes data source, you can choose a data source for Linear customer attributes such as Intercom, Zendesk or Front and create mappings between source tool attributes and attributes on Linear Customers. You can also choose None as a data source to allow more control via the API or directly in the interface. Once an external source is configured the company name and domain are automatically synced.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nOwner, Revenue, Size, Status & Tier can be remapped to pull from data in your source tool. These values  are regularly kept up to date as they will be synced with your source.\\\\n\\\\nYou can also choose to enable manual edits for any attributes that are unmapped. Attributes will always be editable via the Linear API regardless of this UI-specific setting. \\\\n\\\\nBuild customer views\\\\n\\\\nAll customer data brought into Linear can be used to create custom views for both issues and projects to surface the impact of your work on your customers. For example, create a view of issues with requests from Enterprise customers (\\\\\\\"Tier\\\\\\\" property from Intercom), where each issue has at least 20 requests. Ordering this view by customer count surfaces the most highly requested issues to the top. Triangle icons indicating requests marked important surface urgent needs.  Consider consulting this view when planning upcoming cycles to ensure what you ship aligns with the needs of the users. \\\\n\\\\nNotifications \\\\n\\\\nYou'll receive Inbox notifications automatically when new requests are added to issues you subscribe to so you're always in the loop. Optionally, configure additional notification styles for new requests here.\\\\n\\\\nSubscribing to custom views is the best option for more targeted alerts. For example, you might wish to stay aware of issues commonly raised by customers who churn. To track that, create a custom view defined by Customer status includes Churned, and ordering the issues by customer revenue. Click the bell icon to be alerted when a new issue is added to the view, or configure notifications to a Slack channel for greater visibility. \\\\nYou can also open a specific customer page (O then Q) and under the bell icon subscribe to get notified when a request is added, marked important, completed or cancelled.\\\\n\\\\nExport Customer Requests\\\\n\\\\nShould you need to export your customer requests from a particular customer, issue, or project, an option exists in the Cmd/Ctrl + K menu. Search the command menu and select  Export customer requests as CSV to take that action.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}]\"}]}}],\"durationMs\":544}"}}]}

tasks.prompt: List the most recent issues in Linear.

task_result.final_answer:
Here are the most recent issues in Linear:

*   **ENG-277: Support multi-organization membership and org switching** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-276: SDK DX follow-up: unify client + orchestrator entrypoint** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-275: Implement security.txt for api.kontext.dev and app.kontext.dev** (Status: Done)
*   **ENG-274: Add security headers to app.kontext.dev (login)** (Status: Done)
*   **ENG-273: chore: add stricter type-checking and export validation** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-272: Replace elicitation polling loop with event-driven completion notification** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-271: fix(sdk): handle elicitation completion notifications instead of discarding them** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-270: feat(sdk/server): auto-elicitation in server SDK middleware** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-269: SDK: Support connecting to MCP servers that don't use Kontext server-side SDK** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-268: SDK: Multi-server orchestration for KontextClient** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-267: Make integrations tenant-wide by default; remove per-app attachment coupling** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-266: Client SDK: support hybrid routing for gateway + internal MCP servers** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-263: PR-174: Per-user OAuth flow for MCP integrations** (Status: Done)
*   **ENG-261: Server SDK: run Kontext-authenticated MCP servers without the gateway** (Status: Done)
*   **ENG-260: SDK: review integration connect UX + API surface** (Status: Todo)
*   **ENG-258: Organizations: invite & manage additional admins** (Status: Done)
*   **ENG-253: Python SDK** (Status: In Progress)
*   **ENG-250: feat: Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591)** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-249: chore: Check cimd / client.dev / ORY settings** (Status: Backlog)
*   **ENG-230: feat(auth): Add Google and GitHub social login to dashboard** (Status: Done)

tasks.prompt: Search Linear documentation for information about integrations.

task_result.final_answer:
Based on your search, here's a summary of the information about integrations in the Linear documentation:

### Integration Directory

Linear has an **Integration Directory** that lists all available integrations, including those built by the Linear team and third-party applications. You can find it in your workspace settings under **Settings > Workspace > Integrations**. Integrations built by Linear are marked with a star badge. To install most integrations, you'll need to be a workspace admin.

### Key Integrations

The documentation highlights several key integrations:

*   **Zapier:** Allows you to build no-code automations to create or update Linear issues based on triggers from other apps. You can connect Linear with services like Typeform, Gmail, Intercom, Google Forms, and more.
*   **Sentry:** Lets you create and link Linear issues from Sentry error reports. When a linked Linear issue is resolved, the corresponding Sentry issue is automatically closed.
*   **Zendesk:** This integration allows you to create and link Linear issues from Zendesk tickets, making it easier to track bugs and feature requests from customers. It can also automate reopening Zendesk tickets when the linked Linear issue is resolved.
*   **Intercom:** Similar to the Zendesk integration, this allows you to create and link Linear issues from Intercom conversations. It also supports automated reopening of conversations and syncing of internal notes.

### Building Your Own Integrations

You can also build your own integrations using Linear's API. The documentation encourages building applications using OAuth. Once you've built an integration, you can submit it to the Integration Directory by filling out a form. Linear generally accepts integrations that are useful to the community and built by formal companies. For questions, you can reach out to `integrations@linear.app`.

tool_calls.result: {"content":[{"type":"resource","resource":{"uri":"inline://execute-code","mimeType":"application/json","text":"{\"result\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"ce5ff110-a02b-450e-be48-1fcddba7dbc9\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Integration Directory\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/integration-directory\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Discover Linear add-ons or build your own\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nLinear's Integration Directory features apps and add-ons created by the Linear team as well as external applications. Install these to improve your workflow and sync with your favorite tools. You can also build your own integrations and submit them to the directory.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nLinear crafted\\\\n\\\\nIf you see an integration with a star badge on the icon, that means it was crafted by the Linear team. You can install them via the link in the directory or by going to Settings > Workspace > Integrations and then select the integration name. \\\\n\\\\nFor most integrations, you'll have to be a workspace admin to install them for your workspace. If you don't know who is an admin, go to Settings > Workspace > Members and filter for Admins.\\\\n\\\\nThird-party integrations\\\\n\\\\nThe directory also features integrations built by other apps and third parties. We recommend doing your own research into the integration owner and permissions required before installing these integrations. You can find the creator's website and contact in the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nBuild your own\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear's API to build your own integration and submit it to the directory following the instructions below. We recommend building applications using OAuth and having a separate workspace for the application, which gives all admins access to the application (instead of only the application creator).\\\\n\\\\nWe'll accept integrations that we think are useful to the community and are built by formal companies. We generally do not accept scripts or apps built by hobbyists, but feel free to reach out to integrations@linear.app if you think it should be included. You can also ask questions and see what others are building in our Slack community's #api channel.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit your integration\\\\n\\\\nFill out this form. It includes a sample page to give you a sense of copy style and length.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit assets to integrations@linear.app or include a link in the form. We've built a template in Figma to make this easy.\\\\n\\\\nSend any questions to integrations@linear.app\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"26e5ac68-6d40-430d-8c11-4828b2993b6a\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Zapier\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/zapier\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"This integration lets you build custom automations to create or update Linear issues when actions are taken on other apps or using any of Zapier's triggers.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nZapier is a good solution for building no-code automations with Linear.\\\\n\\\\nGetting Started\\\\n\\\\nCreate a Zapier account and then create workflows using our Zapier integration. This integration is open source and you're welcome to contribute to it. Zapier has a free trial after which you're charged based on usage.\\\\n\\\\nYou can create workflows with apps such as Typeform, Gmail, Intercom, Google Forms, Discord, Airtable, Todoist, Productboard and more.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nBuild workflows\\\\n\\\\nOur Zapier trigger can be combined with other app triggers or with Zapier's default triggers to build workflows and automations. For instance, you can set up a Zapier workflow that creates a Linear issue whenever a specific Typeform is filled out. \\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nActions\\\\n\\\\nYou can take the following actions in Linear in response to a trigger you configure in Zapier:\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdate an issue\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue attachment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new comment\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new project\\\\n\\\\nTriggers\\\\n\\\\nIf Linear is the start of your Zap, the following actions can be used as triggers for downstream actions in Linear or other applications:\\\\n\\\\nNew issue\\\\n\\\\nNew issue comment\\\\n\\\\nNew document comment\\\\n\\\\nNew project\\\\n\\\\nNew project update\\\\n\\\\nNew project update comment\\\\n\\\\nUpdated issue\\\\n\\\\nUpdated project update\\\\n\\\\nIssues created through Zapier appear as created by “Zapier” and not the user who authorized the application.\\\\n\\\\nExample workflows\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue with a bug label when you receive an email message with specific keywords.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue when a tag has been added to an Intercom conversation.\\\\n\\\\nLet team members or clients outside of Linear create bug reports and feature requests via an online form.\\\\n\\\\nCreate an issue whenever a custom database query returns a new row.\\\\n\\\\nCreate a dealflow pipeline: use a form integration such as Typeform or Google Forms to create a new Linear issue with a custom description.\\\\n\\\\nUpdate to latest version\\\\n\\\\nIf you're not seeing full functionality, you may be running an old version of the Zapier integration. Update the version you're using in a Zap from the status pane:\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"af0baca0-f66c-439b-93c9-18d4d1a3f898\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Sentry\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/sentry\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Our integration with Sentry lets you create Linear issues from Sentry and create links between Linear and Sentry issues.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nCreate new Linear issues from Sentry exceptions and link Sentry exceptions to existing Linear issues. Once enabled, the option will show up in Sentry under Linked Issues. When a Linear issue is completed, any linked Sentry issues will be automatically resolved. Re-assigning an issue in Linear will update the assignee on linked Sentry issues, too. Read more about the integration on Sentry.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nEnable the integration in Linear from workspace settings. \\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nCreate issues\\\\n\\\\nWhen viewing a Sentry issue, go to the right sidebar and select Create Linear issue, then fill out the quick form. We pre-fill the description with the Sentry issue details and you'll add the title, team, assignee and priority. \\\\n\\\\nLink issues\\\\n\\\\nWhen viewing a Sentry issue, go to the right sidebar and select Link Linear issue, then search for it existing issue by ID or title. \\\\n\\\\nAutomations\\\\n\\\\nWe’ll automatically close Sentry issues when the linked Linear issue is resolved. We'll also update the assignee in Sentry if it changes in Linear (this will work if you use the same email for Linear and Sentry).\\\\n\\\\nYou can also configure automatic creation of Linear issues based on issue/event alerts and metric alerts in Sentry from Alerts > Create Alert and customize your rules to create a Linear issue.\\\\n\\\\nRemove links\\\\n\\\\nTo remove the Sentry link, right-click on the link from the Linear issue and select Remove.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\\n\\\\n\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"d6bed7b3-06fb-4b77-b148-61fb6ecdce52\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Zendesk\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/zendesk\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Our Zendesk integration makes it easier for you and your team to keep track of bugs and feature requests and interact with the customers who report them.\\\\n\\\\nOverview \\\\n\\\\nOur Zendesk integration lets you create Linear issues from Zendesk or link existing issues to your Zendesk tickets. When linked, Linear issue information is shown inside Zendesk and a link to Zendesk is added to your Linear issue. Automate updating Zendesk tickets when its related Linear issue has been closed (Done or Cancelled). Our integration will also re-open the ticket so that your agents will be able to notify the customers.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nInstall\\\\n\\\\nThere are two steps to installing the Zendesk integration:\\\\n\\\\nInstall and approve Linear for your Zendesk workspace by installing it from Zendesk Marketplace. This will add the Linear widget to your Zendesk ticket sidebar.\\\\n\\\\nEnable Zendesk automation from Linear's Zendesk settings page. This will enable ticket re-opening and updates to your tickets. You must be a Zendesk admin to complete this step.\\\\n\\\\nAfter you install the Linear add-on, it will show up in the right sidebar when viewing a ticket. Each agent will have to login to their Linear account from the application to create and view issues.\\\\n\\\\nRequirements\\\\n\\\\nOur integration requires that each Zendesk agent who installs Linear  also has a Linear account, since the linked issues will be created in their name. This also allows agents to open the Linear issue to update it or add more information. This integration is available on our Business and Enterprise plans.\\\\n\\\\nBasics\\\\n\\\\nCreate new issues\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue from the Zendesk widget. It will bring up a form which requires a title and that you assign the issue to a team. Optionally add priority, assignee, or labels from within Zendesk.\\\\n\\\\nWhen linked, Linear issue information is shown inside Zendesk while viewing the related ticket. This also adds a link to the Zendesk ticket from the Linear issue.\\\\n\\\\nOptionally, toggle \\\\\\\"include message\\\\\\\" in the widget to add message content from the customer ticket to the Linear issue description, including images if applicable.\\\\n\\\\nCreate with Linear Agent\\\\n\\\\nIn addition to creating issues manually from a Zendesk ticket, you can use the Create with Linear Agent option to automatically create Linear issues using AI.\\\\n\\\\nWhen selected, Linear analyzes the entire conversation, including customer messages, support replies, metadata, and any attachments, and identifies the underlying product request or bug. The Linear Agent generates an informed title and description while pulling in the relevant context from your conversation with the customer. \\\\n\\\\nA customer request is created when applicable, and the issue is routed to the desired team for review and prioritization.\\\\n\\\\nTo use the Linear Agent in Zendesk, enable the integration in Settings > Integrations > Zendesk and turn on the option to allow AI-generated issues. \\\\n\\\\nOptionally, you can also provide additional guidance to help route issues to the correct teams or templates. This guidance can include examples, team mentions, or internal rules the Linear Agent should follow when interpreting feedback.\\\\n\\\\nUse templates\\\\n\\\\nLinear admins can designate up to 10 templates to be available for quick use from within the Linear integration in Zendesk. Selecting any of these templates will pre-fill issue property fields appropriately, helping to speed up issue creation and maintain a high level of data hygiene in your issues.\\\\n\\\\nLink existing issues\\\\n\\\\nSearch for issues by their issue ID or words in the title to link them. This will add a Zendesk link to the Linear issue. Zendesk's issue search works the same way as our in-app Search.\\\\n\\\\nRe-open conversations\\\\n\\\\nIn the integration settings page, you can automate the reopening of the linked issue when it's completed, cancelled or a comment is made. The integration will post an internal note and re-open the conversation in Zendesk so it's easy to follow up with customers.\\\\n\\\\nLink multiple issues\\\\n\\\\nSometimes customers will write in with multiple requests at one time. You can link as many Zendesk tickets to a Linear issue as you like.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nLink Zendesk tickets in Linear issues\\\\n\\\\nUnlink issues\\\\n\\\\nClick Unlink from the Linear widget to remove the link between the ticket and a Linear issue. You can also remove this from the Linear issue by right-clicking on the issue link.\\\\n\\\\nMerge duplicate reports\\\\n\\\\nIt's common for support requests to relate to bugs or issues already filed in Linear. When you merge duplicate issues that have Zendesk links, we'll move any Zendesk links and comments over to the canonical issue and update the linked Zendesk ticket so that it's connected to the canonical issue and automations work.\\\\n\\\\nClick Unlink from the Linear widget to remove the link between the ticket and a Linear issue. You can also remove this from the Linear issue by right-clicking on the issue link.\\\\n\\\\nFilter for Zendesk links\\\\n\\\\nFrom any Linear view, you can filter by issues linked to Zendesk tickets. Click F then select Links and then select Zendesk.\\\\n\\\\nFAQ\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"673dde96-b4ef-47be-b575-b50fea3ba368\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Intercom\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/intercom\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"Our Intercom integration makes easy to keep track of bugs and feature requests and interact with the customers who report them.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nThe Intercom integration lets you create and link Linear issues from Intercom conversations. Linked issues, their status, and relevant assignee show up in the sidebar in Intercom. Linked Intercom conversations will show up in Linear issues as link attachments.\\\\n\\\\nConfigure\\\\n\\\\nEnable the integration in Linear from Settings > Features > Integrations > Intercom. A Linear app will show up in the Conversation details sidebar in Intercom with options to create issues or link issues. You can show or hide the Linear app in Intercom's sidebar by pinning Linear under Edit apps.\\\\n\\\\nWhen troubleshooting where you uninstall and re-install the Intercom integration directly on the same Linear workspace, comment sync and status updates for existing connections will resume without problems.\\\\n\\\\nUser Access\\\\n\\\\nAnyone in Intercom can create or link Linear issues and see the issue details in the sidebar. Only Linear users will be able to view linked issues in Linear. If the person who created the issue is a Linear member, the issue will show that it was created by them and they'll see the issue under Created in My Issues. Otherwise, the issue will show it was created by Intercom. \\\\n\\\\nCreate new issues\\\\n\\\\nCreate a new issue using the button from the righthand sidebar. It will bring up a form which requires a title and that you assign a team. You can also optionally include a description with additional details from the Intercom conversation, assign a priority, add an assignee or add a label. \\\\n\\\\nIssues created through the integration will be sent to the Linear team's Triage Inbox if you've enabled that feature. The Intercom conversation will show up as a link attachment in the Linear issue.\\\\n\\\\nCreate with Linear Agent\\\\n\\\\nIn addition to creating issues manually from an Intercom conversation, you can now use the Create with Linear Agent option to automatically create Linear issues using AI.\\\\n\\\\nWhen selected, Linear analyzes the entire conversation, including customer messages, support replies, metadata, and any attachments, and identifies the underlying product request or bug. The Linear Agent generates an informed title and description while pulling in the relevant context from your conversation with the customer. \\\\n\\\\nA customer request is created when applicable, and the issue is routed to the desired team for review and prioritization.\\\\n\\\\nTo use the Linear Agent in Intercom, make sure the Intercom integration is installed and connected, then enable the feature in Settings > Integrations > Intercom. \\\\n\\\\nOnce enabled, you can optionally provide guidance that helps the agent choose the right team or template when creating issues. These instructions can include examples, routing hints, or references to internal documentation. The agent will follow this guidance when creating issues, ensuring they land in the appropriate place within your workflow.\\\\n\\\\nUse templates\\\\n\\\\nLinear admins can designate up to 10 templates to be available for quick use from within the Linear integration in Intercom. Selecting any of these templates will pre-fill issue property fields appropriately, helping to speed up issue creation and maintain a high level of data hygiene in your issues.\\\\n\\\\nSearch and link issues\\\\n\\\\nClick the link issue option and then search for it by title or issue ID. Linked issues will be added as links to the issue. Optionally add all or parts of the newly linked conversation to the Linear issue as a comment.\\\\n\\\\nView issue details\\\\n\\\\nFrom the Intercom conversation, you will see all linked issues in the sidebar along with the issue ID, status, and assignee.  Click the issue title from the Intercom sidebar to view more details. From here, you will also have the the option to view the issue in Linear or remove the link with the associated Linear issue.\\\\n\\\\nLink multiple issues \\\\n\\\\nYou can link multiple Intercom conversations to a single Linear issue. You can also link multiple Linear issues to a single conversation in Intercom. \\\\n\\\\nRe-open conversations\\\\n\\\\nIn the integration settings page, you can automate the reopening of linked conversations when the associated issue is marked as completed, cancelled or a comment is made. The integration will post an internal note and re-open the conversation in Intercom so it's easy to follow up with customers. Both closed and snoozed conversations will be reopened.\\\\n\\\\nInternal notes\\\\n\\\\nBy default, Intercom posts an internal note when issues are created, cancelled, or completed. Optionally set the integration to post an internal note whenever the issue status is updated. This feature allows Intercom users to gain additional signal on the issue without context switching away from Intercom. Please note that internal notes left in Intercom do not sync to the associated Linear issue.\\\\n\\\\nLink Intercom conversations in Linear issues\\\\n\\\\nClick on contextual menu icon … to the top right of the issue\\\\n\\\\nClick Add link\\\\n\\\\nClick Intercom conversation\\\\n\\\\nRemove links\\\\n\\\\nClick Unlink from the Linear sidebar in Intercom to remove the link between the conversation and a Linear issue. You can also remove this from the Linear issue by right-clicking on the Intercom link attachment or clicking the … icon on the link attachment.\\\\n\\\\nFilter for Intercom links\\\\n\\\\nFrom any Linear view, you can filter by issues linked to Intercom issues. Click F, then select Links and then select Intercom.\\\"}]\"}]},\"logs\":[\"[log] {\\n  \\\"content\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"type\\\": \\\"text\\\",\\n      \\\"text\\\": \\\"[{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"ce5ff110-a02b-450e-be48-1fcddba7dbc9\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Integration Directory\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"url\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"https://linear.app/docs/integration-directory\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Discover Linear add-ons or build your own\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOverview\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nLinear's Integration Directory features apps and add-ons created by the Linear team as well as external applications. Install these to improve your workflow and sync with your favorite tools. You can also build your own integrations and submit them to the directory.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBasics\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nLinear crafted\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you see an integration with a star badge on the icon, that means it was crafted by the Linear team. You can install them via the link in the directory or by going to Settings > Workspace > Integrations and then select the integration name. \\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFor most integrations, you'll have to be a workspace admin to install them for your workspace. If you don't know who is an admin, go to Settings > Workspace > Members and filter for Admins.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThird-party integrations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe directory also features integrations built by other apps and third parties. We recommend doing your own research into the integration owner and permissions required before installing these integrations. You can find the creator's website and contact in the sidebar. \\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBuild your own\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUse Linear's API to build your own integration and submit it to the directory following the instructions below. We recommend building applications using OAuth and having a separate workspace for the application, which gives all admins access to the application (instead of only the application creator).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWe'll accept integrations that we think are useful to the community and are built by formal companies. We generally do not accept scripts or apps built by hobbyists, but feel free to reach out to integrations@linear.app if you think it should be included. You can install them via the link in the directory or by going to Settings > Workspace > Integrations and then select the integration name. \\\\n\\\\nFor most integrations, you'll have to be a workspace admin to install them for your workspace. If you don't know who is an admin, go to Settings > Workspace > Members and filter for Admins.\\\\n\\\\nThird-party integrations\\\\n\\\\nThe directory also features integrations built by other apps and third parties. We recommend doing your own research into the integration owner and permissions required before installing these integrations. You can find the creator's website and contact in the sidebar. \\\\n\\\\nBuild your own\\\\n\\\\nUse Linear's API to build your own integration and submit it to the directory following the instructions below. We recommend building applications using OAuth and having a separate workspace for the application, which gives all admins access to the application (instead of only the application creator).\\\\n\\\\nWe'll accept integrations that we think are useful to the community and are built by formal companies. We generally do not accept scripts or apps built by hobbyists, but feel free to reach out to integrations@linear.app if you think it should be included. You can also ask questions and see what others are building in our Slack community's #api channel.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit your integration\\\\n\\\\nFill out this form. It includes a sample page to give you a sense of copy style and length.\\\\n\\\\nSubmit assets to integrations@linear.app or include a link in the form. We've built a template in Figma to make this easy.\\\\n\\\\nSend any questions to integrations@linear.app\\\"},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"26e5ac68-6d40-430d-8c11-4828b2993b6a\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"Zapier\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://linear.app/docs/zapier\\\",\\\"snippet\\\":\\\"This integration lets you build custom automations to create or update Linear issues when actions are taken on other apps or using any of Zapier's triggers.\\\\n\\\\nOverview\\\\n\\\\nZapier is a good solution for building no-code automations with Linear.\\\\n\\\\nGetting Started\\\\n\\\\nCreate a Zapier account and then create workflows using our Zapier integration. This integration is open source and you're welcome to contribute to it. claim_scores.claim: The response includes documentation about integrations

claims: The response shows the wiki structure or table of contents
tasks.prompt: Show me the documentation structure for the facebook/react repository.

task_result.final_answer:
Here is the documentation structure for the facebook/react repository:

*   **1 Overview**
    *   1.1 Repository Structure and Packages
*   **2 Feature Flags System**
*   **3 Build System and Package Distribution**
    *   3.1 Build Pipeline and Module Forking
    *   3.2 Release Channels and Versioning
    *   3.3 CI/CD and Artifact Management
*   **4 React Reconciler**
    *   4.1 Fiber Architecture and Data Structures
    *   4.2 Work Loop and Rendering Phases
    *   4.3 React Hooks System
    *   4.4 Lane-Based Scheduling and Priorities
    *   4.5 Suspense and Error Boundaries
    *   4.6 Host Configuration Abstraction
    *   4.7 Profiling and Performance Tracking
*   **5 Server-Side Rendering**
    *   5.1 React Fizz (Streaming SSR)
    *   5.2 React Server Components (Flight)
    *   5.3 Build Integration for Server Components
    *   5.4 Server Actions and Bidirectional Communication
*   **6 Platform Implementations**
    *   6.1 React DOM Implementation
    *   6.2 React Native Renderers
